review_text: Great effort by the school and all staff in making the activities fun and achievable for the children. My child loves to be in the outdoor and we are happy to be able to attend such event together as a family.

review_text:

place_name: Star Learners @ Choa Chu Kang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:

One of the reasons we chose this school is its support for our child’s vegetarian diet. The teachers have been understanding and thoughtful, ensuring his needs are well taken care of, which gives us great peace of mind.

The teachers are caring, patient, and attentive, always taking time to understand each child and work closely with parents. It is reassuring to see the school treat a child’s well-being and development as a shared responsibility between home and school.

Special thanks to She Lao Shi, Teacher Darrice, and Principal Shi Xuan for their dedication, kindness, and consistent support. We truly appreciate their effort and heart in helping every child grow with confidence and care.

place_name: Star Learners @ Choa Chu Kang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: My child feels safe and love going to school everyday. The teachers are very nice and communication with parents is great so we know what my child does or learn in school. Thank you Star Learner! :)

place_name: Star Learners @ Choa Chu Kang Central | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
As a parent, finding the right childcare can be daunting — but enrolling my children here has been one of the best decisions I’ve made. From the very beginning, the staff showed genuine care, attentiveness, and professionalism. My children settled in quickly, and it’s heartwarming to see how much they look forward to going to school each day.

The teachers are patient, nurturing, and truly invested in each child’s growth and well-being. There’s a strong emphasis on learning through play, and I’ve seen my children grow not just academically, but also socially and emotionally.

What I also appreciate is the open communication — I’m regularly updated on their progress and any centre happenings. It gives me great peace of mind knowing they are in such a caring and enriching environment.

Highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a safe, warm, and stimulating space for their little ones!

place_name: SDM Childcare Centre @ Choa Chu Kang
review_text: Leaving our one-year-old was so hard, but the team at SDM made it easy. His teachers are so warm and nurturing. We get updates throughout the day, and nothing beats seeing his big smile at pickup. He's happy, safe, and thriving. We are so grateful!

place_name: SDM Childcare Centre @ Choa Chu Kang
review_text:

I am generally reluctant to visit Choa Chu Kang Polyclinic unless absolutely necessary, such as for my recent ankle injury. Otherwise, I would prefer to visit Bukit Panjang Polyclinic, where I have consistently found the staff to be more professional in their patient care and more responsive to patients' needs. Their appointment management is also noticeably more efficient.

I understand that unforeseen circumstances can arise in healthcare settings and that delays may sometimes be unavoidable. However, what concerns me is the consistency of the long waiting times at Choa Chu Kang Polyclinic. Across all five of my visits, despite having scheduled appointments in advance, I experienced waits ranging from two to five hours from consultation with the doctor to collecting my prescribed medication.

While I appreciate the efforts of the healthcare staff, I hope the clinic can review and improve its workflow and queue management processes. Patients make the effort to arrive on time for their appointments and it would be greatly appreciated if their time could be respected as well. Such prolonged waiting times seem unnecessary and could potentially be reduced with a more effective management system.

I hope this feedback will be taken constructively and contribute to improving the overall patient experience.

Update at 12.20pm: I managed to see the doctor by 12.15pm, completed my X-ray by 12.30pm and collected my medicines at 1.30pm.

A special shout-out to Dr Azlin Azali. She was warm, jovial and most importantly, showed tremendous empathy. The moment she saw me limping into the consultation room, she immediately bent down to assess my condition. Swift, professional and reassuring, she promptly insisted that I be provided with a wheelchair given my situation.

In short, her enthusiasm, warmth and genuine compassion set an exemplary standard for medical staff. Kudos to Dr Azlin Azali. She has certainly earned two extra stars for CCK Polyclinic.

I made an appointment online yesterday and went there today. Would like to shoutout to Dr Azlin Azali for being super nice and kind. As it was relating to women's health, she attended to me gently and tried to lighten the mood given my uneasiness. Very thankful for her kindness and patience. The healthcare assistants were also working hard to manage the queue and attending to the elderly (most of the patients are seniors). I would think I'm quite lucky today and managed to get everything done after an hour post my assigned appointment time. Many thanks to Dr Azlin and her kindness, and kudos to the staff working hard there!

I would like to compliment Nurse Siti AhzuHaira Ahmad from National University Polyclinics. She is extremely professional, patient and attentive. She explained my child’s growth clearly, checked carefully if we had any questions, and even re-measured my child’s height when she noticed something was not right. Her dedication and attitude are truly commendable. This was our best experience so far, and I am grateful for such responsible healthcare staff in Singapore.

Back for a review following week and again, same issue. Appt time at 9am. Waited till 10+am for my turn for consultation. Then wait another hour at pharmacy to collect med.

Horrible clinic to go to waiting time is 1 hour end up drag to more then 2 hours 30 minutes to just see the doctor. Horrible place to visit I rather go private to see doctor. The space is gorgeous too — elegant decor, floral setup, and that green wall makes it feel special. Staff were friendly and service was smooth.

All nice and good taste — would definitely come back and recommend for meals or events and very friendly staff like Sarah and the Chef 🧑‍🍳.

My experience with the NESS course conducted from 20 to 23 April 2026 at ITE College West was deeply disappointing.

As a first-time learner with ITE, I had expected a professional, well-structured, and learner-focused experience. Unfortunately, the overall experience fell far short of that. The course appeared to assume a basic level of electrical knowledge from the outset, despite the published entry requirements being relatively general. This placed learners without such background at a clear disadvantage.

The ZF portal and related learning and assessment experience were also difficult to navigate. The system was at times slow, unresponsive, or unclickable, which made an already stressful process worse, especially when each question appeared to allow only about one minute for response.

There were also issues with the overall delivery and environment. The nearby toilet at Block 6 Level 1 was repeatedly flooded over the past three days, which posed a safety hazard. I personally slipped and nearly fell. The learning exercise materials were given only in hard copy. In this era, soft copies would make revision and reference much easier.

I also found the classroom delivery disappointing. From my perspective, there was insufficient empathy and support for learners who were struggling to follow the material.

Overall, this was not a minor inconvenience but a genuinely poor learner experience. Given the cost of the programme and the standards one would reasonably expect, I hope the relevant parties will review the course delivery, learner support, and overall setup seriously.

place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
I am thrilled to share my experience with Agrader, where my kids attend classes for math and science. This enrichment center has truly exceeded our expectations in several ways.
The center is always clean and well-organized, creating a positive atmosphere that encourages learning.

The educators at Agrader are not only knowledgeable in their disciplines but also exhibit incredible patience and professionalism. They take the time to ensure that each child understands the material, which has made a significant difference in my kids' learning experience.

Since joining Agrader, my children's grades have noticeably improved. The tailored worksheets and quizzes provided have been instrumental in helping them grasp complex concepts and prepare for exams effectively.

I particularly appreciate their unique rewarding system. The bonus points system motivates my kids to put in their best effort, and they love exchanging these points for small gifts. It’s a wonderful way to recognize their hard work!

Their online Everloop system is a fantastic resource, allowing my kids to catch up on any missed lessons and review corrections on their assignments. This flexibility has been invaluable in supporting their learning journey.

Overall, I highly recommend Agrader to any parent looking for a supportive and effective enrichment center for their children. The combination of dedicated teachers, a nurturing environment, and innovative learning tools makes it a standout choice!

place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: AGrader Learning Centre has been a wonderful support in my child’s learning journey. place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
My daughter is currently attending K2 English and Math at Agrader Tuition Centre, and we’ve had a very positive experience so far.

She has always been a little slower in language development, and we were initially quite concerned about her confidence and interest in English. After attending the trial class, we noticed a clear change, she became more engaged, more willing to participate, and even started showing interest in language, which was a pleasant surprise for us.

The lessons are well-structured and age-appropriate, and the teachers are patient and encouraging. They know how to engage young children without putting pressure on them, which really helps build confidence at this stage.

We appreciate the supportive learning environment and are encouraged by the small but meaningful progress we’re seeing. Thank you to the Agrader team for making learning enjoyable for young learners.

place_name: AGrader Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang) - English, Math, Creative Writing, Chinese, Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text:
Our K2 daughter recently joined Agrader Tuition Centre for English and Math, and the experience has been reassuring for us as parents.

She can be quite shy in class and usually takes time to warm up to new environments. However, the teachers at Agrader were very gentle and attentive, which helped her settle in comfortably. What stood out to us was how the lessons were conducted in a fun and engaging way, allowing her to learn without feeling overwhelmed.

After a few sessions, we noticed that she became more responsive during lessons and more confident when attempting questions, especially in English. She is also more willing to talk about what she learnt in class, which was something we didn’t see before.

We are thankful for the caring approach and structured teaching. It gives us confidence that she is learning at her own pace in a supportive environment.

place_name: TutorJohn Learning Centre - Choa Chu Kang Tuition Centre
review_text:
Very disappointing experience with Tutor John Teck Whye.

We were initially assured that my child would be placed in a small group and taught by a female teacher — which was true for the first few months. However, without any communication, they later changed the class structure and teacher, placing her in a mixed group with a male tutor instead.

To make things worse, when we had to stop due to some last-minute scheduling changes, they refused to refund the deposit, citing company policy. While we understand policies exist, a little kindness and flexibility would go a long way — neither of which this business seems to have.

Would not recommend, especially for parents looking for transparency and empathy.

There are better tuition centres in Junction 10 and Yew Tee Point, don’t make the mistake I did!

The library is always an ideal sanctuary for those who appreciate some peace and quiet.
It has been years since my last visit, and it now appears to have expanded, with an entire upper floor dedicated to the Children’s Section.
Clean, well-maintained, and serene, it’s a place where one can truly enjoy a sense of calm and peacefulness.

Choa Chu Kang Public Library at Lot One is a very modern, clean, and well-designed library that is conveniently located right next to the MRT station and inside the mall. The interior feels bright and welcoming, with spacious layouts and comfortable areas for reading and studying

I especially liked the contemporary design touches and the creative displays throughout the library, which make the space feel more vibrant and inviting. Despite being in a busy suburban mall, the library still maintains a calm and relaxing atmosphere

Pleased with my visit here and how clean, modern and spacious the library is. Plenty of seating areas and even a level 5 kids area to entertain the kids. Great selection of books and many assortment of seats to accommodate many people. I also liked that borrowing a book via the NLB app was such a breeze!

Choa Chu Kang Public Library is a well-designed and cozy space for readers of all ages. The library has a good collection of books, magazines, and digital resources, along with plenty of comfortable seating for studying or reading quietly. The layout is modern, spacious, and family-friendly, with dedicated sections for children and teens. It’s also very convenient since it is located inside Lot 1 Shopper’s Mall, making it easy to drop by after shopping or dining. The staff are polite and helpful, and the environment is peaceful — a perfect spot for learning, reading, or simply relaxing.

I think that the Choa Chu Kang Library is very spacious, especially when I walked in. Once you walk around the fiction shelves, you’ll notice that there’s this seating area of 6 chairs around this round table. That’s a good place to work on your computers, study, and read.

Once you see that, just walk towards the window and look left and right, you’ll notice there’s this long table connected to the wall, and multiple people can sit there. That’s a really nice place for reading, studying, and working on your computers. The view is also nice when you look out the window, depending on where you’re sitting though.

They have this specific Malay section of books, and there’s 3 small sections that are kind of curved in, and about 4-5 people could fit in there. Once you walk a bit farther, there’s this section with teen books, and you’ll have to go down a few steps, but it has a really nice section.

Once you’ve passed the teen section, you’ll see stairs going up. If you go up those stairs, you’ll see the children’s section. First you’ll see the family and parenting section; then to the right, you’ll see the children's books.

Overall, I think the Choa Chu Kang Library is nice and spacious, and has a good selection of different books. Nice area for sitting. A good place to read, study, and work on your computers.

The brand new (actually one year old now) Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange nested in the heartland of Choa Chu Kang town. Practically everything here is modern. From the commuter communication systems, to the new buses, to the electronic display of real-time bus information, to the ticket office with its electronic ticketing machines, to the customer service centre to the vendor services kiosk, to the various stalls selling local foods, this is a display of a well designed and constructed bus interchange that serves residential areas around Choa Chu Kang, Yew Tee, Teck Whye, Bukit Panjang, and industrial areas around Sungei Tengah, Sungei Kadut. Stepping out of this interchange will lead immediately into MacDonald’s and a few steps further into Lot One Shoppers’ Mall. A few more steps will get one to the Choa Chu Kang MRT Station and public amenities include Choa Chu Kang Town Council, Choa Chu Kang Public Library and Choa Chu Kang Park. Of course, not forgetting the Choa Chu Kang / Bukit Panjang LRT system.

Choa Chu Kang bus interchange is an outdoor bus interchange, an older style that stays the same but serves its purposes. There are some food stalls, other necessities, and something I don't see that often in other bus interchange: a lot of charging stations, which must be helpful for many commuters when they need it.

Located just at the end of Choa Chu Kang's MRT station and next to Lot 1 mall, it's easy to access and provides bus to favorite attraction like Singapore Zoo etc

If you are heading to the Zoo, you can take the MRT to CCK station and board bus 927 here directly to the Zoo ( about 25 min, 16 stops).
