review_text:
Yummy pancake:

Avoid the hum-jim-peng, the flour is lumpy and hardened when fried.

Totally unpalatable. I must give one star for the food.

Where is the ethic of food seller? This did not even cross the line of passable.

I advise they just focus on making  pancake & drop the fritters.

Approach with caution.

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text: Went to the newly renovated Keat Hong Food Centre and Market on 2 Nov (Sat) and tried the zichar dishes from Hong Kong Street Old Chun Kee. Pretty solid dishes would be back again!

place_name: Blk 253 Choa Chu Kang Market & Food Centre
review_text: Dishonest seller at the kopitiam traditional Bak Chor Mee stall. Owner was alone working as a cashier and cooking the food and intentionally keyed in my noodle type so he could charge me twice for takeaway container (2x30 cents). They also covered the menu dish showing the on-going promotion so that people would order from their regular menu. Noodles don’t taste good and expensive, very soggy. There are many better options around.

Drinks are cheaper here compared to other food courts.
Fish in this set from halal stall was freshly fried which was impressive but I could only taste the 'salt in it. Chillies was good, egg was done to my liking. Ikan bilies were normal.

I ordered char siew roast meat curry noodles from wang wang roast meat store, but received curry chicken noodles instead. The meat was completely drenched in curry, so it wasn’t obvious until I poured it out at home. Disappointing to be charged more but given a cheaper alternative.

The staff at this store selling Ah Ma Curry Chicken were very rude and inconsiderate. I purchased Ah Ma curry chicken with rice and bread before I collected the bowl was 3rd quarter full only portion is very small total I paid $8 and politely requested extra curry gravy. However, the lady refused in a very rude manner and instigating the male cook not to give extra curry gravy. As a result, I returned half of the bread which I don’t have enough gravy to eat along. Still can ask me to go ahead to complain them 😓

Such service staff should not be allowed to operate without proper training. They need to be trained in basic customer service and shown how to be more considerate toward customers. Some even did not wear masks. NTUC management should take action somehow this outlet is lack of service and inconsiderate mostly operate by foreigners.
5 stars for the steamed fish stall. This is my favourite stall and the only one I keep buying from. Huge portion of fish. Super value for money.

I recently visited the newly renovated food center owned by the well-known chain brand, Kopitiam, and I must say, it was a delightful experience!

The food center is clean and airy, featuring spacious seating and large ceiling fans that create a comfortable dining environment. It's a perfect spot to enjoy a meal without feeling cramped.

Food choices are impressive, with a variety of stalls offering delicious options. If you have the Fairprice app, you can enjoy discounts when ordering at the food stalls, which is a nice bonus!

The kopi (coffee) was light, so if you prefer a stronger brew, be sure to order the kopi gao for that thick, rich flavor.

I particularly enjoyed the famous Jian Bo Shui Kueh and Chee Cheong Fun, absolutely loved them! My kids opted for the tosai which was equally good.

Highly recommended for families looking for an affordable and comfortable place to have breakfast. With its great food and inviting atmosphere, it’s definitely worth a visit!

Mixed rice or Cai fan here is horrendous.

$6.10 for 3 veg 1 meat (tau geh, spinach, curry potato and sweet n sour pork). Tau geh was raw, spinach tastes only of oil. Sweet n sour pork and potato was not bad.

$7.6 for 3 veg 1 meat (broccoli, lady’s fingers, long bean [with tiny, almost non-existent pieces of minced meat], and cereal chicken). COLD, oily, overpriced food.

Buy at your own risk.

Additionally, the seats here are incredibly dirty with plenty of food scraps everywhere.

Hakka Yong Tau Foo Stall. Veggies were tough, white carrots turned sour and the beancurd sheets had a weird rubber band taste to it. Couldn’t finish it at all and sent it to the tray area. Will definitely not eat this again. Cost around $8.10. There were some nice ingredients but only if you’re lucky to get the fresh ones. Service was great though.

Newly opened food court at CCK central. Great food variety ranging from Chinese, Indian, Malay, Jap & Korean except vegetarian.秋林老火汤 returned back and am glad they still served reasonably priced soup and claypot chicken and pork ribs. Comfort food for someone who want to have a light meal.

板面鱼片饺子幼面,minced pork & fish ball mee pok from美味 are nice and highly recommended. I will suggest those that are hungry to upsize their order as portion may be small for some.

Chicken rice stall is also selling nice and tender chicken. What surprises me is that they also provide minced ginger for your diy serving which is very generous from the stall owner.

Bibimbap’s ingredients are very lacking especially the chicken meat and egg. I do not see any egg being included and hence not worth the $7 price tag.

Hot desserts that I tried are well cooked, portion and not too sweet. It is worth buying after your main meal.

However the table & seats arrangements are a bit cramped at the centre area of the food court hence you may like to avoid during peak hours. There are non air con seats available which I will recommend for those who prefer quiet and a bit spacious environment.
It is warm & stuffy for seats near Wok of Wonders and Indian.

Lastly the cashless payment system network is also not stable during my last 2 visits and have to pay cash instead. Be reminded to prepare some cash when visiting the food court. There is also no washroom available and you will need to visit them on the 2nd level of CCK centre.

Broadway management kindly look into my above feedback and hope there will be improvements made.

got randomly scold/told off by this rude cleaner auntie for just sitting on the chairs at about 9pm today. we need a space to put our phone screen protector, and at 9pm the foodcourt is quite empty so me and my friend we just sat at one of the chairs outside to put our screen protector. we at no time was loud or being a nuisance. she just shouted over accusing us of eating?! (but this is a food court?) so i replied no auntie, then she say “ this place is not for you to play one hor!!” firstly, as a 34 years old with a baby face, i am truly disappointed by this auntie’s attitude, judging people based on looks.  but we aint eating /making a mess, nor we are playing??. i feel that even thou it is a food court, she shoulnt just anyhow scold people like this. if she want us to leave, she can just say it nicely, and not accusing and scolding us as if we are a nuisance. i know she might have a long day, but it is not right to spread that kind of negativity around just because she is older then us. good luck having this kind of services.

Disappointing Experience at Newly Opened Food Court,Big Appetite

Visited the new food court recently and, unfortunately, it was a letdown from the start. The seating arrangement is extremely cramped—tables are placed so close to each other that it feels uncomfortable even to sit down, let alone enjoy a meal.

Ordered a breakfast set (2 boiled eggs, toast, and coffee). To my surprise, I only received half  of 2 slices toast. Meanwhile, others around me had a full set with two proper slices—confusing and inconsistent. To make matters worse, the toast was cold and soggy, definitely not what you'd expect for a breakfast staple.

Tried ordering murtabak from the Indian stall. After waiting 10 minutes, I checked on the order and was told by the stall owner that he “didn’t know” the status. He apologized and said it would be another 5 minutes—but it took an additional 15 minutes to finally arrive.

Overall, it’s a poorly designed food court with slow service and underwhelming food. Definitely not worth the hype.

Some tasty food can be found here, as shown on photos; last nite tried the 猪肚湯，the Chinese young staff man the stall n customer very well, she greet cust well, explained her product well. She compliment their yam rice is popular n ran out fast; I was lucky it was still available at almost 8pm. She also told me that I can topup soup (once only).
Other things relating to cleaners impressed me alot; they approached customers table with plates almost empty, asking if they could clear for cust; when food court is near closing time, few cleaners out for action, clean dinning table thoroughly including side of tables. See attached photos for ‘cleaners in sctions’ and stalls with delicious food that worth trying 😀😋😍

1. One or two food stalls sells good food. Mix is extreme.
2. The coffee stall Quench! sells very diluted coffee at $1.80!!!! Buy from Toast/ Yakun ( $2) for more value( yakun small coffee is bigger size than Quench! anyway!!!!!). If there isnt queue at drink stall, beware. After my food, I went Yakun to hv  proper cup of coffee.
3. The spot lights at ceiling are a bit hot but brighten up the place. But why tilting the spotlight at your eyes/ face instead on the food ?Maybe it is plant uplighter concept. or for selfie? Gd try but I appreaciate functionality first.
4. One star because food junction used to be unique initially, dare to lead the market, but now just another follower.

I won’t order the udon from the Japanese & Korean stall again. There’s a very strong taste on the soup of ‘before your cook maggi noodles’ - if u understand what I mean. They should wash/soak the udon first before cooking.
The ebi and fish cake taste bad too.

I initially ordered chicken karaage udon because my son loves karaage but it was out of stock. When I asked to change the chicken karaage to something else instead of ebi, she said no. Inflexible.

The fried oyster omelette at Food Junction Lot 1 is TERRIBLE! DO NOT BUY FROM THERE! Paid $8 for a takeaway box and there is so much flour and barely any egg! The flour is thick clumpy and raw. No taste and badly cooked.

Had the Japanese food here and it was pretty tucked in a corner in order to find. Prices were acceptable as compared to other stalls but generally on the higher side. Teriyaki salmon set for $8. They accept capitastar pay and credit cards. Pretty good variety. There is no economic rice stall but Indonesian Nasi Padang.

place_name: JIA LI COFFEE ROASTER (HOME BASED CAFE)
review_text: Discovered this home-based coffee café through an HDB brochure. Tried the Iced Matcha and Coco Matcha—both were well-balanced and flavourful. (Tip: remember to request less ice if you prefer a fuller drink.). Ordering via WhatsApp was smooth and convenient, though there was a short wait along the corridor for collection. Bonus: their cat, Snowy, made the visit more memorable. Overall, a good experience and worth trying if you’re in the area.

place_name: JIA LI COFFEE ROASTER (HOME BASED CAFE)
review_text:
Came across this no fuss home-based coffee spot near my place. Very surprised with all the sophisticated equipments used by owner!

Owner mentioned he actually does all the preparation of the ingredients from scratch. Recommend this place if you enjoy your drink just the way it’s meant to be — simple, strong, and unsweetened.

place_name: JIA LI COFFEE ROASTER (HOME BASED CAFE)
review_text: Tried their new Matcha latte and it is exquisite, made with ceremonial grade matcha

place_name: JIA LI COFFEE ROASTER (HOME BASED CAFE)
review_text: i had a lovely and affordable iced latte from jia li coffee roaster. I loved it very much and will be back

place_name: JIA LI COFFEE ROASTER (HOME BASED CAFE)
review_text: Another coffee gem appeared in Yew Tee?? This is what we need! Bought the Creamy Latte (and it is really creamy🤤) and Osmanthus Mont Blanc.. ngl i prefer this more than the original Mont Blanc😂

Hearty Breakfast Done Right 🍳🥢

Nothing beats starting the day with comforting hawker goodness from a favourite stall — simple, satisfying, and full of local flavour. 😋🔥

Came here to eat Hock Lian Huat ngoh hiang :)
It’s 24/7 and walking distance from Choa Chu Kang MRT. Came here a few times with my queen after my school from Clementi. Convenient :)
The hokkien mee and wu xiang are nice here. However, the fried rice stall is not so nice. The prawn egg fried rice portion is very miserable only 300 over grams!!! The prawns have a frozen smell.

My favorite coffee shop! Can eat here from breakfast to supper.

Since young, I like the beehoon with fried yong tau fu, ba chor mee and hokkien mee.

The other stores came later but very good also, especially the xiaolongbao and can you believe it, they even have sambal stingray now which is insanely good!

So many yummy options in choa chu kang coffee shop!

A 24 hour food court with the usual roasts, ngo hang, mince meat noodles, bak ku teh till late night/ early morning with a nearby car park.  Good for late night snack/meal. But can get quite crowded with high turnover.

Despite some bad reviews, personally found this place quite value for money!

Ordered both the Sirloin $9.90 150g & BBQ BF Steak 150g sets. We preferred the BBQ BF steak over the Sirloin as it was less tough.

📝 Pro tips!!!
• Remove the steak from the hotplate immediately after it is served if you prefer it medium/med rare.
• We fried the rice in sauces provided with the residual heat from the pan n was yummy

+$2 for the drink n fries (fries was hot n good) & had 3 servings of salad.

Overall bang for buck n a fun experience :)
Good steak, juicy and worth the price. Simple place but nice food. Would come again.
Great value for money! The steak is affordable and surprisingly good. Definitely worth coming back.”

First time trying.
Walked by and noticed it’s a new store, so decided to give it a try. The set comes with rice, cabbage, and soup, plus 3 different sauces for the meat which adds nice variety. The original sauce is my personal favourite. You can also upgrade the meal set with a good range of side dish options, and they have pasta options as well. Overall, good value for money and a quick meal :)

Bought 2x 150g ribeye, 2 sides and 2 drinks for almost $60.

Both ribeye was horrible. Tastes NOTHING like meat at all. Feels like a cheap frozen meat with a weird rubbery texture.

There's better affordable options for halal steak other than this place. We trusted google reviews, but left the shop feeling cheated. 🥲

Had our Mother's Day celebration at this restaurant for the first time. Interestingly they served steak but with a twist of side such as Japanese rice. Love the hot stone which keep the steak hot & cook to perfection. The only downside it will get very crowded especially during weekends.

Went for lunch and ordered the sirloin steak. The steak was served on top of beansprouts so it doesn’t get overcooked. The meat has a good ratio of lean and fat, not the best texture but good enough for the price. Need to remove the meat from the plate quickly otherwise it will become well done. Overall very good value for the 9.9 price.

Came in with no expectations and still left disappointed. Steak came to us raw and when I sent it back they microwaved it and it came back drier than bones. There is no passion for food here, come only if you want a fast meal.

Second time going to Steak 99, first time being at JEM. The JEM outlet is way better than this outlet. Steak came to the table dry and stuck to the stone as the stone did not have oil, the rice and cabbage was lower quality than the JEM outlet. But the soup was good. Service was usual.

If you want to eat Steak 99, the CCK outlet is not it. Go to the JEM one.

The staffs are great, very helpful, atmosphere also nice.
We came for late lunch-early dinner time, ordered rib eye 350gr, chicken steak and some side dishes. Chicken steak were great, taste 👍 nothing to complain.
I want to highlight the steak. I ordered 350gr because i want my steak cooked in certain way, but apparently can't.
Should serve the steak at the porcelain plate like any other steakhouse. Its really a waste when the steak just sit in hot stone and become well done. I don't like my steak well done like rock, i want juicy tender steak rare or medium rare. This is not the place. First and last time to patronizing this place for a steak.

Friendly uncle who gives me a taste from a small cup to check if it is the sweetness level I want.
I like their Osmanthus oolong tea, light,fragrant and nice. A wide variety of flavoured bursting boba, look forward to try them all.
Love the homely vibes. It is a good bbt addition to the place.

The collagen rose milk tea was really nice! It was fragrant and not too sweet. Very good for a hot weather or after a long day. The uncle was very friendly and helpful. He let me taste a sample to check that I was satisfied with the sweetness of the drink! The cup size was very generous for the price and the location. Would definitely go back to try the other options 😋

Really nice experience here. The people there were friendly and even gave me a free topping as a student, which was a pleasant surprise.

I tried the white peach oolong tea with grape topping and it was really good, not too sweet with a fresh, natural taste. At S$2.90, I’d say it’s a fair price too. Definitely a place I’d come back to!

The boss uncle was so nice. He let me tried the sample to see if I was okay with the sweetness! The drinks are fragrant and so worth the price, especially during the 1-for-1 promotion. Truly a CCK bubble tea hideout. Would come back again :)

Uncle boss is very friendly. He will make the drink and let you try a sample then you can let him know if it is okay or not he will adjust.
I got osmanthus oolong 1000ml for 3.90.
located inside food inn at cck mrt

Lot One Shoppers’ Mall remains one of the most convenient and well-rounded suburban malls in the west of Singapore. Located directly beside Choa Chu Kang MRT and the bus interchange, it is extremely accessible and continues to be a popular gathering spot for families, students, and residents in the area

The mall has evolved a lot over the years and now offers a very good mix of shopping, dining, groceries, and everyday services across multiple levels. I was pleasantly surprised by the variety of food options available, from casual Japanese dining at Tokyo Shokudo to dessert snacks like Munchi Pancakes, as well as the large Food Junction food court. There are also regular atrium events and roadshows which make the mall feel lively and active

One standout addition is the modern Choa Chu Kang Public Library, which feels bright, spacious, and thoughtfully designed. The mall itself also feels cleaner and more refreshed compared to many older suburban malls, with ongoing upgrading works helping to modernise the space further

Despite not being a huge mega mall, Lot One manages to cover almost everything you need in one place - supermarket, restaurants, cafés, electronics, fashion, library, and family-friendly amenities

Lot One is a lively and convenient shopping destination that serves as a central hub for residents in the Choa Chu Kang area of Singapore. The mall is especially appreciated for its strategic location right next to major public transport connections, making it easily accessible for shoppers, students, families, and working professionals. Its welcoming atmosphere and practical layout make it a pleasant place to visit whether you are running errands or spending leisure time.
One of the strongest highlights of Lot One is its wide variety of shops and services. Visitors can find everything from fashion outlets and electronics stores to supermarkets, beauty services, and bookstores. This diverse mix makes it a convenient one-stop destination where people can complete multiple tasks in a single visit. The dining options are equally impressive, offering a good range of choices that include local favourites, casual restaurants, dessert spots, and quick bites. This makes the mall popular not only for shopping but also for meeting friends and family over meals.
The mall is also known for its clean and well-maintained environment. Bright interiors, spacious walkways, and clear signage contribute to a comfortable shopping experience. Even during busy hours, the space generally feels organized and easy to navigate. Many visitors appreciate that it maintains a balance between being lively and still comfortable enough for relaxed browsing.
Another positive feature is the community-focused vibe. Lot One often feels more personal and friendly compared to larger city malls, which adds to its charm. Regular promotions, seasonal decorations, and events help keep the environment engaging and enjoyable for visitors of all ages.
Overall, Lot One stands out as a reliable, convenient, and enjoyable neighbourhood mall. Its accessibility, variety of stores, and welcoming atmosphere make it a favourite choice for everyday shopping, dining, and relaxation.

Lot One Shoppers' Mall in Choa Chu Kang is a fantastic shopping destination that caters to a wide range of needs. Conveniently located next to the Choa Chu Kang MRT station, it offers easy access for both locals and visitors. The mall features a diverse mix of retail stores, from popular fashion brands to electronics and lifestyle shops, ensuring there's something for everyone.

One of the highlights is the variety of dining options available, ranging from fast food to casual dining, making it a great spot for meals after a shopping spree. The food court is particularly impressive, offering a mix of local and international cuisines that cater to different tastes.

The mall is well-maintained, with clean facilities and a welcoming atmosphere. Families will appreciate the kid-friendly spaces, including play areas that keep little ones entertained. Additionally, the mall often hosts events and promotions that add to the shopping experience.

Overall, Lot One is not just a shopping center; it’s a community hub where you can relax, dine, and shop all in one place. I highly recommend visiting if you're in the area!

Very nice suburban shopping mall, with good connectivity by public transport. Located right next to Choa Chu Kang Mrt and Lrt station, it is so easy to get there and get home. Buses are also conven with bus interchange located just behind it. I feel the mall is bright and cheery And generally well maintained. There is a good mix of lifestyle shops, eateries, homewares and electronic that can cater to all age groups and demographic. I especially like the food hall at the basement where we had our lunch at the Red Ginger. Food was good with many choices to select from. Quality was good and we had a good meal of nasi padang with   curry chicken and veg. We then head up to Harvey Norman to look for an electric tooth brush. We got what we wanted and was happy to be able to pay using our Capita star evoucher. The trip was great and we reward ourselves with a nice Chinese dessert at Food junction on level 4. We like Lot 1 very much and will definitely visit again.

Main mall for CCK people so it’s always packed. Most reviews say the layout is a bit cramped, and it's true. The library is the best part after the renovation, very quiet and chill. Basement food court always no seats during dinner time, better go up to the higher floors if you want to find a place to sit faster.

Yep, that's Limbang Shopping Centre right there in Choa Chu Kang 👋

*Quick facts about this spot:*
- *Location*: Choa Chu Kang Street 51, in the Limbang estate
- *What it is*: A small neighborhood mall that reopened in 2019 after a major revamp. It replaced the old Limbang Shopping Centre from the 1990s
- *What's inside*: Sheng Siong supermarket, Koufu food court, clinics, tuition centers, and a few retail shops. Pretty much the go-to for Limbang residents
- *Landmark in photo*: You can see the Kranji Secondary School sign on the left. The school's just across the road

Limbang is one of the quieter corners of Choa Chu Kang, mostly HDBs and landed property. The mall is small but convenient if you stay around here.

My recent visit to the shopping center brought back memories from 18 years ago when I last set foot here. The decision to stop by for some food was an easy one since I happened to be nearby after their extensive renovation. As I entered, I was pleasantly surprised by the spacious and inviting environment they had created, giving off a welcoming vibe to the neighborhood.

Arriving around 11:30 on a Tuesday, I expected a bustling atmosphere, but to my surprise, the place was surprisingly quiet and serene. It seemed that not all the shops had opened for business yet, contributing to the tranquil ambiance. Nonetheless, the few shops that were open served as a perfect opportunity for some peaceful browsing and shopping without the usual crowds.

New and improved....cleaner and more organised. Needs to bring in more varieties  of tenants/vendors to make it a really busy hub. All facilities are well put into place. Open section for general shops, air-conditioned section for medical/clinics and dentals. Attached to wet market and coffee shop.Very much improved👍👍👍

Limbang Shopping Centre is a recently rejuvenated neighborhood hub in Choa Chu Kang that blends modern retail with a traditional "kampung" feel. Following its 2022 upgrade, it now offers a more diverse range of dining and essential services.

The shopping center has significantly expanded its food options, featuring a mix of local favorites, international chains, and specialty cafes.

​The center serves as a one-stop location for residents to handle their weekly chores and grocery runs.

​The mall's redesign emphasizes social spaces and intergenerational bonding.

Limbang shopping Centre become a very interesting place for dinning .. especially the Thai restaurant.. the food is marvelous and very authentic taste of Thai.. worth waiting. The bosses ( Michele) is very attentive and staff is very friendly 😉

Was at the fish and seafood section around 7 plus in the morning with many people waiting for their fish to be weigh and clean. Giant management should at least put in 1 more staff to help the staff that handle all this alone.
I want to praise Dinesh the staff on duty that handles all alone. He was very efficient,fast and well mannered during all this busy period. Good job Dinesh! 2 thumbs up 👍👍

Few months ago I went there wanted to buy fish, frustrated and waited for 30 mins but the staff never come to weigh it, his colleague said he was working but dont know where was him. Gave up and left.
Today, I was in rush to buy fish and thus, instead of those fresh one which has to weigh and wait for the staff to clean it, I chose those that already weighed and packed with price sticker pasted on racking. But when reached home, found the fish was never been cleaned at all. End up I had to take longer time to do it myself. Common sense if you put on the racking should be already done processed so people can grab and go. Just like how NTUC, Prime and Ustar do it.
The staff never do their job properly. Super lazy.

OCTO Jet Contractor who handles the trolleys are a big problem; cannot leave at Deck 2 trolley bay to take back $1 coin! Must push back to Level 1. Worker makes problems worse by turning trolley around to take out the $1 coin!  Giant should terminate OCTO JET and get a more reliable and responsible Service Contractor! I'm not against employing Senior Citizens to do the job (I'm 70 this year). But it makes us customers very frustrated!

Hi , i went to Giant Chua Chu Kang today 19/10/25  , as 20/10/25 is Deepavali, Festival of Lights .. I notice that ONLY one sign at the entrance of the supermarket say happy deepavali, the whole supermarket without any decoration or song played and NOT welcoming, I believe Singapore 🇸🇬 will decorate at least the minimum decoration, as I see such Giant is a big supermarket and patronized by all race and disappointed to see such thing happening

Disappointed with GIANT Supermarket authorities

CCK resident

Not sure if giant gives their staff 0 work. The self check out counter staff keeps pestering people to buy plastic bags. Why ? If we need it we will ask for it. She hugs it like it's her money anyways.

Even when I brought my own bag she keeps pestering. Ignored her and point to my bag for her to finally give up.

Giant please. It's just plastic bags don't need to have a staff there to keep asking if we need to buy it.

FairPrice at Lot One Shoppers’ Mall is a moderate-sized supermarket that is convenient for residents and shoppers in the area. It carries a decent range of daily groceries, fresh vegetables, snacks, and household essentials, making it a practical stop while visiting the mall

During my visit, it felt like the supermarket was undergoing major renovation or upgrading works, so some sections appeared a bit transitional and less polished than usual
Auntie with mid-length wavy hair in a ponytail & wearing a jacket, working on 2 April was very helpful! Helped me find my Milo Pandan Waffle, thank you so much ❤️

Fairprice is a great supermarket with all the necessities. first time at this outlet this year and the smell of the fresh seafood/fish area is really bad. it stinks so rushed grocery shopping. selection and variety at this outlet is good though.

Very bad staff. While waiting for customer to do payment and leave (near closing time), I always hear them busy gossiping about their colleagues very loudly (to the extent that everyone could hear their conversation).
This gave a very bad impression of this outlet… I don’t see such things happening in other outlets. If there is a 0 star option I will definitely give a zero star!

This supermarket is so cramped!! Why are the customers been squeeze like sardine during peak period! Can Vipul Chawla, Group Chief Executive come see for yourself. What a messy experience, i really pity your customer shopping here, it sucks!

Absolutely horrible experience. My friend went to buy lunch and the staff treated her like trash for no reason. The lady was insanely rude and even grabbed my friend’s wallet and poured all her coins out like she had no respect at all.

When my friend decided she didn’t want to buy anything anymore because of the horrible  attitude, the staff got even ruder and took some of her coins and didn’t return them. So my friend left with no food AND missing money.

If this is how she behave, I can’t imagine how bad the rest of the staff is.

Would NOT recommend this place at all. Rude, unprofessional, and basically stealing. Never coming back.

If I could would have gave zero totally horrible

my friend and i bought buldak noodles, then we wanted to share food. however, one of your employee service was SO BAD that she didn’t even allow us to have 1 pair of extra chopstick. it’s just 1. not like we were going to put it up our nostrils. and i was unaware but she took my food into the 711 store for chopsticks. if i am not wrong, she was unaware i was holding the chopsticks in my hand and she js brought my food in to ask for extra chopticks. then, i am not sure how one of your employee found out and she came out of the store and yelled at us. like i can agree it’s not my friends fault. it’s js a misunderstanding. i also have to say that she tossed the chopsticks at us and she was very rude. pls CONSIDER my letter here and talk to her about it.

p.s.: her name is daisy

Haggard-looking middle aged lady was visibly annoyed when she had to scan my items. Her reaction was distasteful and disrespectful. My bad for shopping at this particular 7-11 😒

Update: Dropped by this morning and the lady in glasses was very polite and friendly. The difference in service compared to the rude female employee was like night and day. 🤷🏻‍♂️

I am very dissatisfied with one of your female staff members. I don’t know her name, but she appears to be around 35–45 years old and is likely working the night shift. On April 14th, around 8:00 PM, I visited your 7-Eleven store to buy some water.

This isn’t the first time I’ve encountered this, but her service attitude is extremely poor. She doesn’t even say “thank you.” I bought two 1.5L bottles of mineral water and requested a paper bag to help me carry them home, as I was also holding a handbag.

To my surprise, she simply threw the bag onto the counter and continued doing her own things. That was incredibly rude. I’ve tolerated this multiple times, but this time I’ve decided not to stay silent anymore. If your store does not take any action to improve this, I will escalate the matter to your management.‼️‼️‼️‼️
The aunty working on 20 Nov 2025 6+ pm was really rude and scummy. Worst service among all 7-11 I've went. What a rude person that I left without buying anything and wont come back in the future.

The Indian lady is very rude to customer.
I was a bit under the weather after the long day work, the Indian male staff asked me to use self service payment while he was serving another customer.
The Indian lady is very terrible, I knew how to use the machine, you no need to be rude and show attitude to me.
She is not suitable to work as customer service related work if she doesn't like it. Customers are not your emotion dustbin.

I visited this Cheers outlet today and a staff named Tuan was very helpful and friendly. Unlike the other outlets I have been too where the staffs often look moody, Tuan served me with a smile and was so polite to customers.
I would patronize this outlet again, Tuan deserves a pay raise!
Bought bun from Cheers at Lot One this morning for breakfast. The taste was a bit sour and didn’t feel fresh. Just wanted to highlight this so the store can look into the food quality

13 march 2025

Giving a feedback because I didn't get a refund from self kiosk and the employee,the machine didn't state how much was the drink but it was on offer for $1.30 and paid $1.50 and the kiosk refresh and my $1.50 become $0 and the price of the 100plus become $1.90 and I have to pay extra $1.90 and when I ask the the employee he say pay again and I didn't ask again because there are a lot of people in cheers and didn't even get a receipt,only got a qr code.Got a bad experience now only going to 7-11 instead in the future as they have better service and promo

Ellooo,
I’d like to give some feedback.

Earlier on 20Oct25, I just enter the shop to purchase a drink and the staff — an Indian lady — acknowledge me & told me please make fast what you want to buy. I stood there & shock , told her I just came in & you told me make a fast one due you wanted to dispose your rubbish .

This caused unnecessary confusion and unhappy for Customer. I believe customers should at least be acknowledged, and they should wait due other 10min the kiosk going to close. So can't the staff wait awhile when customer wanted to purchase some goods.

Thank you.

This place is a surprise for me. It looks underwhelming from the outside and I practically haven’t heard of it (well, no one would tell me Oi there is this cool wet market here), I usually look for them myself but this one escaped my radar despite me being familiar with this area.

The wet market is not too big, but it’s also not small. However, some stalls are very impressive. I personally really like the corner seafood stall because it’s big, and they practically have almost everything. The merchandize looks fresh as well, the display is interesting and enticing. The vegetable stall, especially the one next to a spice shop also has the best display of fresh vegetable. The spice shop seems to have varieties of products where we can walk in to view it. It looks tradition, dim, but I know this is the kind of place where we can find secret spices for the kitchen.

The floor around the meat and seafood area was wet when we were there, which brought down the ambience quite a bit, but other areas were fine. Price wise, I don’t know much because I didn’t intend to shop when I found the place, and asking around without buying is not a recipe for a good day in Singapore. However, I will come again with a full intention of stocking up the grocery.
Quite disappointed after renovation , by the way I only come every weekend to buy fresh flowers

BEWARE

A fraudulent fruit stall is in Yew Tee Market, manned by an elderly person.

After we selected and gave the banana for billing, the vendor changed to near rotten bananas inside the bag while picking up other fruit .,

One stop for fresh seafood with freshness assured. Cleanliness of the market can't be compromise. Quite clean rest room within d market vicinity but at time the people using it to be civic educated. For wheel chair bound should not be a problem ...

Generally it is a good market. The only stall I avoid is the one and only fruit seller. Rude uncles with bad quality fruits. The yong tau foo is the most popular and all the aunties selling vegetables are very friendly. I like the fact I can buy small amount that I need for the day.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Choa Chu Kang Centre
review_text:
Cozy gym. Decently big too, one side are the machines, cardio, and functional area, the other segment is the dumbbell area, racks, 2 powerlifting platforms (combined with racks), cable jungle.

Came at off peak hour so a lot of equipment available. Some of the equipment could use some oiling though, the tricep pushdown bar was squeaking like no tomorrow

place_name: Anytime Fitness Choa Chu Kang Centre
review_text: Irene is very patient with us, friendly, approachable, and also thoughtful. She will attend to each and every one of us to make sure our posture is correct and won’t injured ourselves. I love yoga classes conducted by her. I even joined her yoga classes at Hwi Yoh CC and Bukit Panjang CC :)

place_name: Anytime Fitness Choa Chu Kang Centre
review_text: Very clean gym. Staff are also very friendly, they do walk the grounds and communicate with gym users asking for feedback and assisting them if needed. Machines are maintained, in the event one spoils they get it fixed up fast. Would love to use a pull up and dips assist machine here.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Choa Chu Kang Centre
review_text: I was at the gym last Saturday for a quick workout before meeting some frens for lunch at Lot 1. My first impression was it was pretty small BUT pretty well equipped. But the gym had a very strong scent. Like that of a male cologne. It would be nice coming from someone passing by but definitely not in an enclosed gym w people working out. Perhaps i have a sensitive nose. But i came in all well but my nose started to get blocked n i wass deadlifting w a dripping nose. Escalated to a sorethroat and fever. Maybe it was coincidental but i believe a milder scent works better? But im here more to actually compliment their staff Piyo for going the extra mile for service. AF CCK is not my home gym but she chanced upon my post on thread about the strong scent in the gym and took the initiative to DM me and check on me. She apologised for the inconvenience though she did not have to and even offered some compensation. But i declined cause i was more flattered by her kind gesture. Thank you piyo for checking up on ur clients/patrons and going the extra mile for servic; for ur patience and kind words too. Its people like u who makes ppl like me keep coming to the gym without feeling inferior n insecure. Thanks again Piyo!! ❤️

place_name: Anytime Fitness Choa Chu Kang Centre
review_text:
AF CCK Centre is a great spot for anyone looking for a clean and well-maintained gym. The space is always tidy and they have a wide range of equipments targeting the different muscle groups.

Their staffs are also really friendly and outgoing which makes it a comfortable workout environment. This gym outlet is a must-visit for those who prioritize cleanliness and convenience during their workouts

Gym is crowded with noisy teenagers on weekdays and weekends almost at all times of the day. They hog the machines as a group.
These schools should have their own gyms!

The ladies toilet with the toilet bowl stall always smells bad.

In terms of neatness, conduciveness and cleanliness its worse than years ago. They should really enforce responsible usage of the gym and hire staff who actually maintain and check the equipment.

They don't maintain their machines enough: 1) Rubber handles on triceps machine are fallen off 2) missing weight labels on some 3) Ab crunch seat height is difficult to adjust up

Ever since this gym is frequented by secondary school kids the equipment has been spoiling and out of service often. The management should consider limiting how often these kids can visit the gym. They're wrecking the entire gym ecosystem

Most people never bring towel but staff no say. Didn't bring once staff confronted. Today observed an old staff following two young girls around the gym insisting them to show their towels. Seems like it's their first time. One of them brought their towel but the staff said something like it must be 30cm or such, so far the rule only said towels is mandatory. Staff could perhaps talk nicely instead of harassing them, if he wants to implement the rule, do it to everyone and not to people who are new to the gym only. There are a lot of guys at the gym without towels, why didn't the staff confront them? If they wish to check on towels, check on everyone and not be bias. I hope the staff can improve their approach

Went to recre the enabling gym.. Quite a full range of equipment. Crowded place but the staff (Johnson and a lady) were very welcoming . Main issue we have here is that the public transport is too inconvenient especially when it rains.

Meals might be a problem at this sports complex. Not much choices.

Good place if you drive
All round activities. accessibility is a bit of an issue. Need tot take feeder from Choa Chu Kang Interchange

Visited recently — honest take. The place feels dated and awkwardly designed overall. The layout isn’t intuitive; navigating between the football field, running track, pool, gym and food outlets (Pizza Hut/Subway) feels clunky and lackluster. Facilities exist, but many corners give off a tired, old-school vibe rather than the polished community hub you’d expect. Accessibility could be better planned too — signage and flow between spaces could use a rethink.
That said, it’s functional and covers most basic sports needs, but it doesn’t inspire much excitement. Feels like a mid-tier community centre that’s been limping along since the early 2000s without a serious refresh.

This 2 post is very risky for the public,
The SPA is badly  maintain and I jet is not functioning. However, the staff is very friendly and helpful. If not because of the bad maintainance, I will give 5 stars

Choa Chu Kang ActiveSG Sport Centre is a fantastic and convenient spot for the neighbourhood. I mainly use the swimming pool here—it’s clean, well-maintained, and usually less crowded than other sport or community centres, which makes it great for a peaceful swim.

The gym is spacious and well-equipped, and there’s also a large hall with several badminton courts and even tennis courts. Facilities are well-organized, and booking through the ActiveSG app is smooth and hassle-free.

It’s also very accessible—there’s plenty of paid parking available, and it’s easy to get to by public transport as well. Definitely a great place for anyone living nearby or in the West.

The pickleball courts at Chua Chu Kang ActiveSG are fantastic! The facilities are well-maintained, with clear markings and good lighting for evening games. Plenty of space for both beginners and seasoned players to enjoy. Booking is straightforward, and the staff are friendly and helpful. A great place to stay active and have fun!

First time to the swimming pool after long absence, maybe,5 plus years. Facilities are newly renovated , flocked by sports lovers, young and old. Will be frequently visiting the pool for a relaxing lap. Saturday and Sunday have drawn more patronizers throughout the whole day compared to weekdays.

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text:
Friendly staff, lovely 50m lap pool, have been coming here for a good 16 years and will keep coming.

The only issue and big issue is that time to time, the lightning alert will be triggered for no apparent reason, when there is no lightning risk or heavy rain, disrupting our experience.

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: I was there on Sun morning. It wasn’t crowded. However I noticed the cleanliness of the pool is not up to standard. The water was like salty, there was like couple of phlegm floating here and there. The smell of chlorine wasn’t strong like the rest of the swimming complex I’ve been to. This has to improve especially I noticed a lot of kids swimming there.

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: Decent place for a family swim as it has a wave pool and a slide. Quite accessible through bus 307 and 979. Good place for nearby residents in yew tee to swim. The place is generally quite clean and during weekdays swim classes are conducted here. Overall had a Great experience swimming here with my family. Will come back again

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text: Love this pool! Has a slide, interactive kids area, jacuzzi, sheltered pool. Pool guardians and guards are friendly and attentive too. Less crowded too!

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Swimming Complex (The Swimming Place)
review_text:
Opened after renovation. Downgraded from previous featured pool to regular pool so the entry fee is also cheaper.

#Update on Jul 2024, the pool is now back to Featured pool as the people told me to purchase Featured pool ticket to enter. I did not go swimming that day, so I can not confirm it, but update here in order not to mislead from my earlier comment.
Our 1210 show was delayed until 1245 & also required a hall change. Luckily we were compensated with a complimentary pass at the end.
Quite cozy just that the halls are abit smaller compared to other Shaw theatres. I recommend picking seats nearer to the back for the best viewing experience as screens are elevated quite high.

Shore Theatre (Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Shopping Centre): A Convenient and Comfortable Movie Experience (4/5 Stars)

Rating Breakdown:

• Service: 5/5 (Friendly and helpful staff, went above and beyond)

• Ambiance: 4/5 (Clean and comfortable, good air conditioning)

Overall: 4/5

Donkey and I were in the mood for a movie treat, and we decided to cycle from Punggol to Jurong Jem for the movie.  Unfortunately, we found out that the cinema was closed down. We decided to head to Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Shopping Centre to catch a movie at their Shore Theatre instead.

When we reached the theatre, it was a small counter, but the lady server was very kind and helped us pick a movie time that was closest to what we wanted. We got there around 6 PM, and there was a movie starting at 6:40 PM.

After the movie, a really nice worker from the theatre guided us to the lift because the escalators were only going up, and we needed to get our trifold bike down to level 1. He even pointed out the exit that was only for theatre patrons. We good experience in this theatre.The air conditioning within the theatre was just right too.

Shaw Theatres, Lot One is a convenient neighbourhood cinema located right in Choa Chu Kang, perfect for residents who don’t want to travel far for a movie. The cinema has multiple halls of moderate size, offering a cosy movie-watching experience. While it doesn’t have IMAX, the sound system and screen quality are still very good for regular screenings.

The seats are comfortable, with enough legroom, and the halls are kept clean.
The staff are polite and efficient, making the ticketing and snack counter process smooth. Being located inside Lot One mall, it’s easy to grab a meal or do some shopping before or after the movie, which adds to the convenience.

Bad experience today even though movie experience was good but snack counter staff and experience was very bad  bought a chicken bites n drink and went inside movie n ate 2 - 3 pieces and found it was undercooked and i almost felt nausea n bad stomach thro whole movie  and i closed the box i couldnt go back due to movie was imteresting m dont want to miss it.

After movie i went back to snack counter n told them what hapoened, there was no sorry and they took snack n refried it n gave back i told them movie is over n spoiled my experience and they starred n say nothing. Once im home i asked my family to try it well once again it was undercooked

Very fun to climb and there are many children here playing at night time. It has 3 different climbing structures for different age group of children to play. It uses criss Cross rope for children to climb to the top and slide down. It is a great way for young children to expend their energy and it is a convenient location to reach by bus and a 10 minute walk from MRT

Nearest carpark is at Block 408. No nearby toilet facilities as it is still under construction.

There are 2 playgrounds in this compound which is quite well maintained.
Pirate ship playground is for under 5 years old and the other more challenging one is for 5 to 12 years old.
For the size of this playground, you can easily spend about 45 mins to an hour with your kid there or even more.

Nice spacious outdoor playground with tall structures for kids to climb and run about. Probably good for 5 year olds onwards! We enjoyed ourselves there. If you forget to bring water or food, there is a 7-11 about 3 minutes away.

This is certainly one of the biggest playground in the island! The major features are catering to bigger children (ie 9 to 12 year olds). The various towers, tube nets and high slides are truly great fun. HDB should consider such playground for the newer estates and not just catering to young kids.

Fun, clean, free and huge with ample slides and obstacles to let the little ones vent out their energy!
Finally a huge open playground concept in the West area...
They are also building a multi purpose hall with toilets facilities. A secured Futsal court and a basketball court,

just been there today ! opening hours are 8am to 2pm and 4 to 10pm!
great free mini-water play area for kids to cool off. great for preschoolers, my 4yr and 6yr old enjoyed themselves! has mini slides, water sprays and mini overflowing buckets. good enough.
its under the mrt tracks , and mostly shaded!
Its rather fun for kids and convenient for parents. Except that theres no proper rinsing and changing area. Only toilets in the cc at 1st floor. Otherwise have given 5 complete stars.

My 2 yo had loads of fun at the playground. It was a bit cleaner than I'd expected given how publicly located it was, but still a bit disgusted by some cigarettes butts lying around on the ground especially since kiddos are gonna be running around barefoot 🤢. Would it be possible to put clear "no smoking" signs or a cctv to deter people from smoking there?

Amazing waterpark! Although a fraction of the size of other neighbourhood water parks, this works for West-siders😍 Take note that the park is closed from 2-4pm. Do check park timings!

There are seats for parents/guardians. Partially sheltered. To those sitting at the front of the playground there is chance of water being splashed at you! The water jets can be of high pressure.

Overall, would recommend this place. With Starbucks and a Burger King beside, it’s definitely a place for splashing family fun, Lot one is nearby! You can have dinner there. There is a toilet but no shower area, only to rinse off.

Thanks to Keat Hong CC for arranging this waterpark, hope to see many more😍
Suitable for kids below 3 yo. There is a shower area for rinsing but no toilets nearby except in cc. There are seats in the pheriphery of the water park as well.

🌿Choa Chu Kang Park is situated opposite Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange along Choa Chu Kang Drive.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️It's a community garden within the Choa Chu Kang Estate where residents gather for morning workout, walk, jog and do some gardening.
🌿This green oasis offers a relief to the hectic city pace.
🛝🐕‍🦺🍆Like many community gardens, CCK Park too have its fair share of Playground, Fitness Corners, Dog Run, Allotment Garden, Open Lawn and Amphitheatre.
🛼🛹🌶️Besides these regular features, CCK Park have a Skate Plaza and a Community Garden.
🍂Also, somewhat hidden from view, is an Old Tomb Trail.
🚍How to get here:
Exit from Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange to Choa Chu Kang Drive.
Cross over the road.
Park is next to Shell Station.
Some parts under remodeling.  Over all still good for visiting. Quite, shady with Macedonia just outside SAFRA, and walking distance to  Lot 1 and CCK MRT.

The park is clean, well-maintained, and very peaceful. It has lush green lawns, shaded walking paths, and plenty of seating areas. The atmosphere is refreshing and ideal for morning walks, yoga, or spending relaxed time with family and children. Overall, it’s a great place for fitness, leisure, and connecting with nature.

Can an urban park be a space for relaxation, education and creativity? Can it become a unique attraction of the region? Or maybe you are looking for a business idea and want to create an outdoor eductation center? Bet on an interactive park of experiences!

Families are increasingly looking for unusual forms of recreation. When traveling on a trip, they want to not only visit the beautiful old town or museum, but also to provide their children interesting experiences, combined with education. Experience Park is a place where children will not be bored!

The open-air museum will provide great entertainment and learning through play. You can touch each exhibit, run it and learn about the laws of nature through the individual experimentation.

Until now, city parks were equipped with benches, fountains, and nature monuments. Recently, you can also work out in the outdoor gym. It is worth to equip this space with educational devices and in addition to muscle training, practice the mind as well! We suggest creating an educational path, which may consist of several thematic zones, located in different parts of the park.

Small park with lots of ants, wouldnt recommend to come for a stroll either day/night. Most people come here for Mr Teh Tarik Cartel that's located inside the park itself (this would definitely recommend).

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Central Mini Road Safety Park
review_text: 10/10 cute little park to learn about road safety. There's a pebble path to blast your feet if you're into that

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Central Mini Road Safety Park
review_text: This is a fun mini park that is reminiscent of the road safety park in East Coast. Good option for people who stay in the west and north!

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Central Mini Road Safety Park
review_text: Abut more shades from trees will be nice. Maybe a make shift bus stop too? :)

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Central Mini Road Safety Park
review_text:
Great spot for little ones to cycle or scoot!
Great park with a playground, fitness corners and bball court nearby too

Would be great if there is a toilet nearby as well

place_name: Choa Chu Kang Central Mini Road Safety Park
review_text: Good small road safety park with seating space for parents while kids play
Many birds and nice places to explore  nature. About 2km+ with proper walking path. Must bring insects repellent, many many mosquitos
Kranji gate is better reached by cycling or driving as roads do not have sidewalks

Super quaint and ulu place where most of the visitors are bird watchers (run into a few during the visit). Quite hard to reach because closest bus stops (let alone train stations) are kilometers away. Walking to the location is also hard since the road has no pavement. The upside is that it's free of charge and the observation tower at the end of the trail gives a nice vantage point over the bird reserve and Kranji Reservoir nearby. Maintenance works could've been better since some of the sheds seem to be quite run down.
Very quiet place with few visitors. Smell of animal poo, probably some animal farm nearby.

Kranji Marshes offers a unique escape into nature, but it's a mixed bag in terms of maintenance and experience. Located in Singapore, the marshes are a good option for those looking to immerse themselves in a quieter, more natural environment away from the hustle and bustle of the city.

The walking trail is decent, making for a pleasant trek, and I found that a 30-minute walk provided an opportunity to appreciate the surrounding flora and fauna. However, the lack of upkeep was evident. Some paths were overgrown, and the area felt somewhat neglected, which detracted from the overall experience.

One significant downside was the mosquito situation. I ended up with 18 bites during my visit, so I recommend bringing insect repellent or wear long sleeves and pants if you decide to go. The marshes can feel quite deserted, which might appeal to some seeking solitude, but it could also be a bit unsettling for others.

Overall, Kranji Marshes has potential for a refreshing nature walk, but be prepared for a few inconveniences along the way. If you are willing to overlook the maintenance issues and the pesky mosquitoes, it can be a worthwhile retreat into Singapore's natural landscape.
nice place but visitors didn't take care
Great park but the place is always filled with litter.

Nice little garden with decent amount of facilities and play area.

Open field patch could have been bigger and flatter, while the wooden floors could have been better maintained when you could see patches of rotten blocks breaking off, endangering joggers and cyclists.

Very well conceived neighbourhood park, with rain garden, lawn, variety of play and exercise area for different age groups, natural landscape, even taking local history into account. A good example of biophilic design. Hope this could be a benchmark for other HDB estates.
Most of the chairs especially the made of wood have lots of ants and they are not well take care of.so tiring when ur kids are playing and parents have to stand.

​"I used to love the wooden badminton courts at Keat Hong CC, but they recently updated the flooring (around 11 June 2026) to a wood-style carpet overlay. It looks great initially, but it is incredibly slippery. A few other players complained about it, and after playing a couple of games, I felt it too. It definitely doesn't feel safe to play on. I really hope management considers changing it back to the original flooring!"
Came for the STUDY PODS don’t have, remove le but they left tables at the place the PODS used to be. Sad

Large CC, mostly go there for the pod, easy to access for those who stay nearby

Lots of pods but with free WiFi unlike other pods and plenty of tables at same level if pods are unavailable

There are so many wrongs about Keat Hong CC despite it only being built in 2018.

1) There is only ONE road leading into this building complex despite the map showing otherwise. Design issue?

2) Main office is now undergoing RENO/Repair? after barely 3years plus from opening. How bad were the design or material used initially??

3) Access to 3rd Floor and above can only be via LIFT which is slow and you guessed it... breaks down often! Fire access stair means no way to take the stairs. Another design fault?

4) Tap in the sink area are also broken. Only 1 is working (confirmed for 1st and 4th floor).

5) Restaurant Pho Street AC is down as well. CC responsibility or Restaurant issue?

Located near Choa Chua Kang Mrt, this Community Centre serves the residents around the vicinity. It has five levels, which includes a bowling alley, a gym some tuition centres and food options. The Community centre is quite well maintained but cleanliness of toilets could have been improved. Nice decorations for different occasions.

Excellent Park Connector, I love watching people going for a jog whenever I have Guard Duty on weekends. It makes me feel like I'm protecting them and makes me feel like I have a purpose. 5/5 star no drama, will watch this park connector via the cams again.
Good connection between park connector, park as well as the upcoming CCK SAFRA.
This park connector connects Cho Chu Kang Park and Pan Sua Park Connector
Who says taking kids out costs money? A free outdoor playground lets your child have a blast without spending a penny! Perfect for dog walking or strolling.

place_name: Little Footprints Preschool @ Choa Chu Kang - Limbang
review_text:
We’re very thankful for the caring and dedicated teachers at this preschool. Our daughter has grown so much in confidence, independence, and social skills since joining. The teachers are patient, nurturing, and always keep us updated on her progress.

It gives us great peace of mind knowing she’s in a safe and supportive environment every day. Thank you for giving her such a wonderful start!

place_name: Little Footprints Preschool @ Choa Chu Kang - Limbang
review_text: Very good teachers and staff here. My child has a good learning experience here. Highly recommended.

place_name: Little Footprints Preschool @ Choa Chu Kang - Limbang
review_text: It has been totally a great experience for my daughter in little footprints Cck. Teachers and principal are always forthcoming and friendly to parents and responsive with parents query. Happy to have my girl graduated from little footprints Cck successfully!

place_name: Little Footprints Preschool @ Choa Chu Kang - Limbang
review_text: We’ve had such a wonderful experience at Little Footprints Preschool @ Limbang for all of our four children. The staff is incredibly caring, and the curriculum is both engaging and nurturing. Our kids have grown so much socially and academically during their time here, and we truly appreciate the supportive environment that made them feel safe and excited to learn every day. We are sad to be transitioning to a new school due to our move, but we’ll always remember the positive impact this preschool has had on our family. Thank you for everything!

place_name: Little Footprints Preschool @ Choa Chu Kang - Limbang
review_text: I'm thoroughly impressed with LFP CCK commitment to academic excellence and student growth. The teachers are passionate, supportive, and dedicated to helping students succeed. The curriculum is engaging, and activities foster creativity and teamwork. I've seen significant improvement in my child's confidence and grades since joining this school. Highly recommended!

Ace@work BLK 757 provides excellent childcare. The teachers are both responsible and friendly, creating a safe and nurturing environment. Children are engaged in stimulating activities. It’s a centre where parents can have genuine peace of mind. Highly recommended for its caring and professional approach.

Very thankful to have trusted our old neighbours’ recommendation to take a leap of faith to send our kids to Ace@work since 7-8 years ago when we just moved into the CCK/YT neighbourhood… after the eldest got enrolled, following the younger and subsequently the youngest… think we would have walked this path with Ace@work for over a decade long. 🥹🥹

Although we did not mingle much with the current principal , the previous longstanding one - Ms Emily Chan, had really held the centre well together!

This school charges decently and provides quality education and child care support, you can entrust your children in the care of the dedicated teachers. The teachers are patient, nurturing, supportive, good at crafts, dancing , singing and excellent at planning events that surpassed many expectations. Academically balanced with spelling as preparation from K1… and also made toilet training for the kids successful.

The student-teacher ratio and also turnover of teachers at this branch is relatively low, infection control is pretty well done too - low rates of infections disease outbreak (they do regular spring cleaning).

Some teachers really serve with great passion… you can feel it in their communication & the genuine care that they have for your child… special mention of Teacher Pauline, Teacher Lala, Teacher Mae, Teacher Mala, Ms Loh, Wang Laoshi, Li laoshi… grateful to the hardwork put in to put up amazing graduation performances.

Keep up the great work in childhood education!
Special thanks to Miss Loh, Teacher Mae, 王老师 and 李老师, always patiently take good care of my daughter and teach her a lot of knowledge. From PG to N1, we are very satisfied with everything.

Child care centre and preschool educational centres with curriculum specially designed for uniquely holistic comprehensive approach. Caring skillful professional warm teachings for future generations. Conveniently located at Limbang neighborhood Hartland with walking distance to MRT station and bus stations. Plenty of local delights shopping banking and amenities nearby. Friendly fast attentive cosy comfy welcoming ambience for students teacher families locals visitors and Expats.

Hats off to all teachers, assistant teachers and principal. They are doing a great job in nurturing and providing the structure and guidance my kids need. The level of feedback and overall parents-teacher communication is impressive. I am contended and at peace to know that both my children are in good hands. Thank you so much for your dedication and love you all are putting in for our kids.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang Yew Tee Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Student Care Tuition Singapore
review_text: We are very happy with the student care centre and truly appreciate the dedication of the teachers. They are responsible, patient, and care for the development of the students. We’ve seen positive changes in our child. Thank you for creating such a nurturing and structured environment for the children.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang Yew Tee Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Student Care Tuition Singapore
review_text:
My daughter has been attending EduFirst Yew Tee Branch since Primary 1, Term 3, and we’ve seen a noticeable improvement in her energy, confidence, and performance across all subjects. The teachers are dedicated and supportive — they make sure she is well-prepared before every spelling test, which really helps to build her foundation and boost her confidence.

We also appreciate the care they provide beyond academics. She always comes home with a full tummy, and she often tells us how much she enjoys the food provided — a small but meaningful touch that shows they care about the well-being of the students.

Thank you to the EduFirst Yew Tee team for creating such a nurturing and effective learning environment!

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang Yew Tee Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Student Care Tuition Singapore
review_text: My two kids joined EduFirst Yew Tee branch at the beginning of the year, and I’m pleased with the safe and caring environment.  The teachers are attentive and supportive, and all the staff are friendly and caring. I also appreciate the thoughtful services like CCA pick-up and extended hours until 8pm, which have been a great help for us working parents.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang Yew Tee Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Student Care Tuition Singapore
review_text: There are quite a lot of students in the class, so sometimes my son’s homework is not checked. However, the teachers and staff are good and friendly. I appreciate that the teachers are consistent in giving spelling on time and also update parents on the children’s clock-in time, making sure they are safely picked up. Overall, the centre is good and systematic. :)

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Choa Chu Kang Yew Tee Branch) - English Math Chinese Science Student Care Tuition Singapore
review_text: For months, we had been unsure about how to help our child prepare for local school admission. Then we found EduFirst, where Teacher May provided a study roadmap that put us at ease. Watching our son gain new skills and a sense of pride in his work has been a wonderful experience.

as all other mrt in singapore, yew tee is disabled/wheelchair friendly and yew tee square and yew tee point is a stone's throw away. easily accessible to convenience stores, NTUC supermarket, there is a fruit shop just outside the mrt station which sells a variety of fruits! overall clean and well maintained.

无法表达的情味, not about the look but rather the vibes. yewtee is special not because of physical built environment but rather the lack of it. Being often labeled as the worst precint in Singapore, the reality is that the lackluster environment is what makes yewtee shine. The location of Yew MRT station is considerably superior. It is reasonable to live nearby. Have numerous stores near the MRT. Two fast food outlets in a hawker center lot (Subway, macD, stuffD). It's a very casual neighborhood shopping mall with a bus station and mrt and Lrt (train) station right next to it. You can find, food court and fast food options (with a few other restaurants). It also has a library, book store, flower shop and a few clothing stores. Very convenient place to stop by if you're in Choa chu kang or at the mrt station.

place_name: S-11 Choa Chu Kang 787 Food Court
review_text:
S11 covers the basics well — clean, spacious, and easy to move around, with good circulation space so it doesn’t feel cramped even during busier periods. The layout is practical and comfortable for a casual meal.

Food highlights & notes:

Yew Tee Fen Zen Lor Mee stands out — genuinely tasty, well-balanced, and worth a try.

The Indian Muslim food stall consistently draws a long queue, especially during peak hours, so expect a wait.

Tried the fried black carrot cake ($4.30) and fried kway teow ($5.30) — both were decent, but flavours leaned more towards the sweeter side, which may or may not suit everyone.

Black coffee at $1.20 is good value — straightforward, no frills, and acceptable for the price.

Where it falls short:

Food quality is uneven across stalls — a few good ones, many fairly average.

Seating can be limited when crowds build up.

Ambience is functional rather than memorable — pleasant enough, but not a destination.

Overall:

A reliable, clean neighbourhood spot with some decent food options if you know what to order. Does the job, but nothing that really wows — hence a fair 3 stars.

place_name: S-11 Choa Chu Kang 787 Food Court
review_text:
Rating the new mini zichar shop right at the entrance of the food court.
Very affordable prices for decent portions, taste wise allright.

place_name: S-11 Choa Chu Kang 787 Food Court
review_text: Tried the Indian rojak. Nice and yummy. The rojak gravy is good 😊

place_name: S-11 Choa Chu Kang 787 Food Court
review_text: It is good to see that there are quite a few coffeeshops in the vicinities of Yew Tee MRT station, not just next to the station. The food may not be great but it is readily and conveniently available. That is the most important point.

place_name: S-11 Choa Chu Kang 787 Food Court
review_text:
There are 2 stalls in this open air food court that serve food that i like. The first is OE Salad. The salads served are fresh and has a a great variety of ingredients like cranberries, guava, chick peas, pineapple cubes, almond flakes on top of the usual carrots, lettuce and corn. The combination of these ingredients results in a refreshing and delectable experience when eating the salad. Comes with choices of meats like grilled salmon or teriyaki chicken if you want to have them at an additional price.

The other stall is the lor mee style. Its thick flavorful gravy and tasty ingredients that coats the thick yellow noodles makes it an enjoyable experience eating it

place_name: Blue Owl Café (Yew Tee — Takeaway Coffee)
review_text:
I recently tried the coffee from Blue Owl Café (Home Based – Takeaway ONLY) in Choa Chu Kang, and it was a wonderful experience. Despite being a home-based setup, the ordering and pickup process was smooth.

The coffee itself was excellent! Rich, aromatic, and well-balanced without any bitterness. You can tell care was taken in selecting and brewing the beans, as the flavour was clean and satisfying even without sugar. It’s the kind of cup that coffee lovers will truly appreciate.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for quality coffee in the area. It’s a hidden gem that proves great cafés don’t always need a storefront to deliver a great brew. ☕

Nice setup outside the doorstep, just pick and go!

place_name: Blue Owl Café (Yew Tee — Takeaway Coffee)
review_text:
Tried a cosy home-based café recently and really enjoyed it. Their iced matcha latte was creamy, smooth, and really nice. The place is super cute and thoughtfully decorated, which makes it feel very welcoming. The owner was also really friendly and easy to talk to, which made the whole experience even better.

A lovely hidden gem! I would definitely recommend and can’t wait to try their other drinks soon!

place_name: Blue Owl Café (Yew Tee — Takeaway Coffee)
review_text: Ordered after lunch on Thu 11 June on take.app.. Really frenly boss & wonderful yummy drinks.. We had an Aerocana, Iced dirty matcha, Hot matcha latte & Cappucino (shared btw 3 adults..) Cute coaster & clips gifts (clips hijacked by e kids so not in photos 😅)..

place_name: Blue Owl Café (Yew Tee — Takeaway Coffee)
review_text: I tried their iced matcha latte with lavender honey and it was so so good. My initial order was not available so they were super helpful in helping to arrange an alternative that I would like and it turned out to be absolutely delicious. Refreshing, creamy and not overpoweringly sweet. The lavender honey added another dimension of flavour without tasting artificial. Great midday pick-me-up and the area was also decorated really cutely and cozy. Would definitely order again :)

place_name: Blue Owl Café (Yew Tee — Takeaway Coffee)
review_text:

The drink was rich and full of deep chocolate flavor, perfectly balanced and not overly sweet! Perfect combination for a Friday tea break in the office 😊

Everything seemed fine when I got home, but later that evening I realized that one of my items was missing. I suspected that I might have accidentally left it behind at FairPrice Finest.

I returned to the store that same night and was relieved to learn that the item had been safely kept by the staff. I am truly grateful for the honesty, integrity, and helpfulness shown by the team.

A special thank you to Hu Shiyun for the excellent assistance. Experiences like this remind customers why great customer service matters. It was reassuring to know that my lost item was handled with care and kept safe until I returned.
