If you have ever been to parks in Punggol, Sengkang, Bishan, Ang Mo Kio, Bukit Batok or Bukit Panjang, etc., you will know that this park probably ranks the lowest or worst in Singapore, it's just sad and miserable.

🌳Yew Tee Park is located at a corner of Choa Chu Kang North 6 and Choa Chu Kang Way.
🌿The hillside neighbourhood park is a green lung for the residents of Yew Tee and Choa Chu Kang estate.
🏃‍➡️The squarely shaped Park has a tartan track that wound around the Park's perimeter where residents jogs round it during their morning workout.
🏫At the Park's Hilltop Plaza, residents can be seen doing their morning routine exercises and workouts.
🛝Over at another corner of the park is a Children Playground.
🏀🧑‍🦽‍➡️🍖Other features around this hillside Park includes a Basketball Court, an Elderly Fitness Corner and BBQ Pits with two Pavilions complete with benches and washing basins.
🌳This hillside Park is well shaded with many matured tall trees making it comfortable to be around even in the midday.
🚀Fun facts:
Yew Tee was formerly a village off Woodlands Road.
During the Japanese Occupation, oil was stored at this village.
The village became known as Yew Tee "油池".

We recently visited Yew Tee Park Playground on a sunny weekend afternoon, and it turned out to be a hidden gem for families—especially if you're out with a toddler and just looking for a few hours of fun that’s both active and safe.

From the road, the park looks like a quiet grassy hill. But once you step inside and follow the footpaths up the slope, it opens up into a delightful little adventure zone built right into the hillside. We loved that it wasn’t a huge or overwhelming space—perfect for younger kids to explore freely while staying in sight.

The first thing that caught our eye was the hilltop obstacle course that leads to two tower platforms. These towers are fun to climb and had enough challenge to make it exciting for older kids too. We chose to skip the first tower because it had a bit of a fly problem at the base (nature, right?). The second tower was much better maintained and gave us direct access to the elevated obstacle bridge—our toddler found it thrilling to cross while holding our hands.

The highlight of the visit was definitely the camouflage slide. It’s a long, gentle slide stretching all the way down from the upper terrace to the base of the hill, with a cool army-style green pattern. The design is clever—not too steep, not too fast—so even our little one could ride it safely and confidently. We did it a couple of times just for laughs!

There were also swings with safety harnesses, which is such a thoughtful addition for toddlers. These swings go faster than you'd expect, which made it all the more fun for both the kids and the adults taking turns.

What made this park especially enjoyable was how it combines natural terrain with active play features. You don’t feel like you’re in a man-made playground—you feel like you’re climbing through a jungle fort, sliding through a secret tunnel, and bouncing through an outdoor adventure. There’s also a sheltered pavilion at the top of the hill which would be a lovely resting spot or even a place to bring lanterns during Mid-Autumn Festival.

There are no large crowds or loud noises—just a peaceful, family-friendly space for toddlers and parents to play together, surrounded by greenery. If you're in the Yew Tee or Choa Chu Kang area and looking for a relaxing, wholesome way to spend an afternoon with your kids, this playground is definitely worth checking out.

Yew Tee Park is just minutes away from where I stay and it is where I normally go for my runs. The wonderful running track around the Park is about 450m long. The trees provide ample shades for my runs. There is also one of the longest slides I have seen in Singapore, and very popular with the kids. Lots of other facilities amidst the serene, well used and well loved Yew Tee Park.
Very nice and quiet park for people to gather and children to have play. Amazing play ground that are very few in Singapore. One hidden gem in the neighbourhood.

Love this small but delicate park. The slide is super fun and my kid keeps on playing over and over again. There are resting chairs under the shelter which is very nice for parents to take a rest. And there’s also a huge grass land and maybe the kids can play football too. A really good part to visit.

Beautiful park. Great place to hangout with family and just enjoy the evening breeze. The view is spectacular. Although the park is quite small, it has a Basketball court, Multipurpose outdoor area, a Community Hall, Shelters, playgrounds and more. Some of the equipment is quite old but still worth visiting or just having a walk here.

🌳Located at a 10 mins walk from Yew Tee MRT Station, Limbang Park is a neighbourhood park where residents of Yew Tee and Choa Chu Kang frequent regularly.
🏀⚽The Park features a basketball court cum Multi-purpose hard court and a futsal court.
🐒🐯🐷🐰There is an interesting mini suspension bridge, and a zodiac animals walkway.
🤹🧘The Park have 2 children playgrounds catering to different age group.
🏋️🚶Other features include fitness corners, foot Reflexology Path, rest shelters, jogging paths and more.
🍄Fun fact:
Limbang is "balance" in Malay. It is derived from a old road, Lorong Limbang.
Yew Tee consist of two towns, Yew Tee and Limbang.
Limbang is the smaller part of Yew Tee town.

A nice park to take a leisurely walk or exercise at. There are various facilities in this park which include exercise corners, a basketball court, as well as a street soccer court. It is also a place that is frequented by avid Pokemon Go players as there are two poke gyms here which are both potential EX raid locations.

Great place to visit for recreation and leisure. Has a basketball and futsal court, a few small exercise areas, a nice track and a playground for kids. Recommended hours are before 10.30am and after 4.30pm. The sun can be rather unforgiving in the noon.

Good location for family bonding and elderly to walk the parks with Fitness corner.

It is a good place for to bring your pets out for a walk.

Traffic road conditions are really bad. Two way traffic with both side vehicles parked. (Congestion and high chances for accidents.)

No proper car park or parking space.

review_author_name: David Chee (Sun Chaser 太阳追行者)
review_text:
A small quaint n serene Park complete with outdoor exercising machines to do your daily outdoor exercises !
Definitely a place for those opting for a quiet n non-intrusive place to workout ! !

Beautiful park, intelligently designed and built under the expressway. Features playground, dog run, sand pit, kids obstacle course, a little stage, exercise areas, community garden, kids “roads” for practicing traffic rules, lots of nice benches, toilet, vending machines and lots of nicely designed greenery. Connected to rail corridor. Nicely sheltered from the elements by the expressway above.

Of course you will initially hear the constant buzz of the expressway above, but after a while your ears will just tune out.

Photos don’t do this place justice - the place is much more stunning than photos can show !

Quiet neighborhood park with a relaxing vibe

Villa Verde Park is one of those small community parks that’s simple but refreshing. It’s clean, well-kept, and peaceful — perfect for a quick walk or some quiet time after work.

There’s plenty of greenery, open space for light exercise, and benches where you can just sit and relax. The area feels safe and calm, with families, joggers, and kids playing around.

It’s not a big park, but it’s the kind of spot that gives you a break from the noise of the city. I like going here to think, stretch, or just enjoy the breeze.

Nice place for nearby residents who want a quiet and relaxing corner without going far.
Brand New fly over. Can cycle through as well. Link to Chua chu kan Park. There is also a very modern looking exercise corner.

Pang Sua PCN is my regular route for my morning walks before work, and it's a fantastic green artery in the neighbourhood. It's a well-maintained path that's perfect for a bit of exercise and a dose of nature.

​It's especially lovely in the early morning as the sun is rising. The path is surrounded by lush greenery, and the canal is often calm and reflective, as you can see from my photos. As the morning progresses, it becomes a lively, bustling shared path, so you'll see plenty of other walkers, joggers, runners, cyclists, and PMD riders. It's great to see it so well-used by the community, but it does mean you need to be vigilant and considerate, as everyone shares the same space.

​The real magic of this PCN, for me, is the surprising amount of wildlife. A particularly interesting spot is the area near Sungei Kadut, where the MRT track passes over the canal. It's a great spot for "train watching" as the trains go by, and this specific area also seems to be a hotspot for the local fauna. It's here that I've frequently spotted herons and egrets diving for fish, and the large monitor lizards (like in my video) seem to frequent this part of the canal. Elsewhere along the path, you can also see squirrels dashing along the railings and geckos on the fences.

​A separate highlight and a favourite rest stop for me is the Pang Sua PCN Fishing Deck. It's a well-designed wooden deck that juts out over the water, offering a lovely, unobstructed view. Even if you're not fishing, it's the perfect tranquil spot to pause your walk, catch your breath, and just enjoy the serene view.

​Overall, this is a solid 5-star park connector. It's a vital, lively, and green space for exercise, and the abundance of wildlife makes every walk interesting.

My go to place for riding, offers roughly 3.8km from end to end (sungai kaput to junction 10) for runners and cyclists. Has 1 steep slope around the centre and an underpass to allow for seamless connection throughout the entire PCN. Pedestrians should bear in mind to keep to the left and avoid blocking out all your senses to look out for traffic (not looking around, deafened by music).

It was a scenic walk. Bring an umbrella or poncho incase it rains which it did for me. As per my experience, certain cyclist were travelling at higher speed than recommended at the pathway which could pose as a danger to pedestrian. As it is a shared path for cyclist and pedestrians, be careful of cyclist that might be reckless. Be sure to be mindful of shared track etiquette such as keep left and give way to pedestrians.

Nice, mostly flat park connector to run at (about 3km long) with toilet facilities, a water dispenser and vending machine at the rest area under the Yew Tee flyover. Other than that, not many sheltered rest areas.
From Sungei Kadut to Bukit Panjang Post Office, it is about 3.6km with one traffic light crossing. Good for run. Just need to keep to the left for own safety as cyclists are frequent.

We’ve had a very positive experience with this preschool. The teachers are caring, patient, and genuinely attentive to the children. From the beginning, they made the effort to understand my child’s personality and needs, which gave us a lot of reassurance as parents. The environment feels safe, welcoming, and well-organised, and the daily routine helps children settle in comfortably. Communication with parents is clear and timely, and we always feel informed about our child’s progress. Most importantly, my child is happy to go to school and has grown more confident and independent. We’re grateful for the dedication of the teachers and staff.

We feel so lucky to have our kids at Little Footprints Preschool @ Keat Hong 803. My son joined the school since N2 and is now already in Primary 6 — he had such a pleasant and happy time here that we decided to send his little sister as well when she turned 3. A huge thank you to MsJaz, the principal, and the amazing teachers – Teacher Angela, 邱老师, 郭老师, Teacher Diana, Teacher Ann, 孙老师，and all the other teachers who have guided and cared for my children over the years. They are always so patient and kind, and my kids truly love them. Every day they come home happy and excited to share what they’ve learned, which says so much about the love and effort the teachers put in. We’re really grateful for the warmth and dedication of the whole team.

On this special occasion of Teacher’s Day, we would like to express our heartfelt gratitude for the remarkable work you do each day. Your dedication to teaching and nurturing young minds is truly inspiring.
Enrolling our daughter in Little FootPrints Preschool (KH803) has been one of the best decisions we’ve made. The school has not only provided her with a structured environment for learning but also nurtured her social and emotional growth. Over time, she has become more confident, independent, and joyful in her learning journey.
We are especially thankful to the dedicated principal, Ms. Jaz, the caring teachers (Teacher Angela, Ann, Rianne, Bel, Diana, 邱老师, 孙老师, 郭老师, and 黄老师), as well as the supportive staff who have each played a vital role in her development. Your patience, encouragement, and guidance have made a lasting positive impact on her life.
As we celebrate this day, please know that your hard work and commitment do not go unnoticed. You are deeply appreciated, respected, and loved by your students and their families.
Happy Teacher’s Day!
The principals and teachers at LFP are patient, caring and put in tremendous care and effort into caring and guiding the children. Lovely to see how the children blossomed over the years!

My experience with Little Footprints Preschool at Keat Hong 803 has been fantastic! The educators are nurturing and genuinely care about each child’s growth and development. My child has shown significant progress in learning and social skills since enrolling.

A special mention to all the teachers and staffs, for their dedication and kindness in creating a warm and engaging environment for the children.

I truly appreciate the school’s efforts in fostering a love for learning through creative activities and hands-on experiences. The emphasis on parental involvement also makes us feel like part of a supportive community.

I highly recommend this centre to any parent looking for a safe, fun, and enriching preschool experience for their child!

We have had a wonderful experience with little footprint. What stands out most is how the teachers encourage independence through a structured daily routine.
A huge thank you to Fu Lao Shi and Teacher Sakthi for the incredible care you’ve shown this term. It’s been wonderful to see how much my girl has grown in her independence and confidence under your guidance. We truly appreciate the way you both create a supportive environment where she feels comfortable expressing her feelings and exploring her creativity.

I would like to express my heartfelt appreciation for the care and dedication shown to my daughter. She joined LFP @ Keat Hong 805 when she was in N2 and at that time I was concerned about whether she could adapt to a new environment as she had always been shy and fearful of unfamiliar people.

To my pleasant surprise, after 11 months she has grown more confident and audacious, often sharing with me about her activities in school with great enthusiasm. It is clear that she is in good hands, the teachers are very responsible and attentive.

I would especially like to compliment her class teacher-Teacher Ivy, for taking such good care of my daughter. Her guidance and support have allowed me to focus on my work without worry, knowing my child is well looked after.

Once again, thank you to all the teachers and staff at LFP @ Keat Hong 805. You have done an excellent job and I truly appreciate your commitment to nurturing the children.
Teachers are very patient and passionate about their work. They are very professional and caring towards kids of all ages in school.

I would like to express my deepest gratitude to all the teachers at LFP @ Keat Hong 805. In the 10 months since my son joined, he has blossomed from a little baby into a cheerful, caring, and confident young boy.
A special thank you to his class teacher, Teacher Fu, for her incredible dedication and patience. She cares for my son from the bottom of her heart. We are so grateful to have her guidance during his playgroup journey. Thank you, Teacher Fu and the entire team, for your hard work and commitment! Our son  'Ai Ni Oo' loves you! ❤️❤️❤️

Been with them since my 2nd born till  now(4th born is K1 now). So around 8years+. Their teachers are all very nice and helpful. Can really connect with parents & the kids.Really recommend this centre
Attended ACJC's traditional biennial funfair. Such an exciting event! Kudos to all the students and teachers who made the event so grand.

Not only does this school love sucking out money from its students to allegedly maintain facilities that 1. students NEVER get to use and 2. NEVER become cleaner or better in condition, they also enjoy developing bladder diseases in their poor students.

Teachers, especially the discipline master, ridicule and punish, sometimes even forbid, students from simply using the toilet. A 5 minute trip to the toilet to empty one’s bladder from holding it in for hours is seen as an act of sin, a crime being committed by this school that praises itself for being so loving with the grace of God.

During chapel, assembly, or school events where students are gathered in the hall, using to the toilet is seen as a threat, a crime. Students will either be held captive in another room where they will not even be able to see what is going on in the main hall to appreciate the event, will be told to take the route that is much longer to come back to the hall (which makes absolutely no sense as one would have to walk past much more people if their reason is disturbing the course of event), or simply, told no and scolded for “not being able to hold it in”.

Teachers say to students in a humiliating, condescending tone that if they cannot hold their bladder for more than a few hours, they should “get it checked”. This is either implying that students should not stay hydrated or using the toilet is undesirable, when it is part and parcel of how the normal human body works. Over time, with students not being able to use the restroom when they urgently need it, kidney and bladder diseases may be manifested. Urinary Tract Infections are amongst one of the many diseases students would have a much higher chance of having after holding in their urine for extended periods of time just to adhere to some school expectations. Not only that, it is simply extremely uncomfortable sitting still with a full bladder.

I sincerely hope the school will be more considerate of students’ health and needs and be more understanding.

I went to ACJC this morning at 11:30am as I wanted to purchase a swimming paddle for my child. At the entrance, the security guard Mr. Liao asked me to register and requested for my IC. I simply asked him whether it was necessary to provide my IC, but instead of explaining politely, he responded in a rude manner and told me not to “talk too much,” threatening not to let me in and even challenging me to complain to the authorities.

I believe such an attitude is not appropriate, especially within a school environment that should set an example for students in communication and respect. If students were to overhear such an exchange, what impression would they be left with?

I sincerely hope the school can look into this matter and ensure better communication and professionalism from the staff. Your improvement will be very much appreciated.

Wonderful Junior College providing a holistic education. Tremendously benefitted from my experience here at ACJC in 2003-2004, with lifelong friendships and bonds still going strong 20+ years later. Blessed to be part of the ACSian family, with teachers and even our former Principal being ever supportive of our individual endeavours. The sports facilities were fantastic too from the track to the swimming pool and gym, I credit a lot of my sporting journey from this too. Thank you, ACJC! 🙏🏻

The location is strategic, as it sits between 2 mrt stations, and surrounded by many eateries and shopping malls. The sport complex is big, there's football field, running track, swimming pools, as well as badminton court.

Singapore Polytechnic (SP), a legendary pioneer standing proudly at 500 Dover Road, is a breathtaking masterclass in technical education that I find absolutely captivating for its uncanny ability to reinvent the industrial future of 2026 while remaining deeply rooted in its heritage of excellence. Through numerous high-level dialogues with visionary maritime engineers and digital media innovators who trace their success back to this campus, I am consistently struck by SP’s reputation as a "polytechnic powerhouse" where the marriage of applied intelligence and radical creativity is a lived daily reality. Based on glowing testimonials from close associates who have thrived within its specialized clusters, it is evident that the institution operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where students are empowered to master complex aeronautics or sustainability systems under the guidance of mentors who are genuine titans of industry. Public sentiment remains exceptionally high, viewing this Dover landmark not merely as a school, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in Singapore’s engineering, life sciences, and business sectors. Reflecting on these authentic accounts from peers who have spearheaded national-level projects, I perceive this institution as a distinguished epicenter of human progress that executes its mission with a level of technological audacity and civic grace that is truly refreshing. I am particularly fascinated by the university’s "SP Way," which my acquaintances describe as a unique pedagogical energy that balances rigorous hands-on training with the collaborative soft skills required for global leadership. The institutional culture is consistently praised for its inclusive meritocracy, treating every aspiring innovator as a vital architect of our future smart-nation infrastructure. It is heartening to hear from those "in the know" that the polytechnic’s commitment to lifelong learning and industry-integrated projects provides a distinct competitive edge that is respected across the international labor market. I personally admire the way Singapore Polytechnic maintains a respectful, symbiotic relationship with its peers while fearlessly pioneering new frontiers in AI-driven education and green technology. Every conversation I engage in regarding this institution reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and professional transformation. Those who have navigated its rigorous curricula describe it as a transformative journey where youthful ambition is meticulously refined into elite technical expertise within a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. Ultimately, aligning one’s career trajectory with Singapore Polytechnic is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to lead the 21st-century technological transition with confidence and integrity.
Amazing! It is great place for the student. I visited for a mentoring session and it was a great experience.

Singapore Polytechnic honestly feels like its own ecosystem. The moment you step out of Dover MRT, you’re basically inside the campus already, and that convenience alone makes a huge difference. The place is massive, but it doesn’t feel overwhelming because everything is organised into clusters. Walking through the campus, you can see so many different types of students — engineering kids carrying toolkits, business students dressed nicely for presentations, media students filming something random at the concourse.

The school has a very “industry-ready” vibe. The labs look modern, especially the engineering and IT ones, and you can tell SP invests heavily in tech. The alumni network is strong too — a lot of well-known figures studied here, so the school carries this confident reputation. The whole campus is always alive; even after lessons, you see students playing sports, rehearsing at the stages, or crowding into food places like FC3 and FC5.

What I liked:

Super clean and modern environment — nothing feels rundown.

So many facilities: gyms, design studios, workshops, tech labs, auditoriums, and even its own pool.

Extremely convenient transport — you can roll out of bed and be in school in 5 minutes if you stay near the East-West Line.

The culture feels mature, almost like mini-university life.

What could be better:

Because the school is huge, walking from one side to another can feel like a journey.

Popular food places get packed extremely fast during lunch.

Some of the older blocks are showing age, but still in good condition.

Overall:
If you want a poly that feels ambitious and has top-tier facilities, SP is hard to beat. It feels like a place where people genuinely get things done.

Easy place to access as its near Dover mrt. May need a student pass to get thru the gantry. I was there to attend a public workshop and so the helpful security assisted me. Many students I met to ask for directions were very helpful. There are a few Food Courts (air-conditioning and non air-conditioning) plus varieties of food at reasonable price. As the venue is huge, one need to walk (take it as part of exercise). O
Present for graduation day a well known polytechnic in Singapore

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Keat Hong) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: I’m very happy with my daughter’s progress since joining the English tuition centre. She has become more confident in speaking and writing, and her vocabulary has improved a lot. The teachers are very patient and make learning enjoyable, which keeps her motivated. Thank you for guiding her so well.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Keat Hong) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: Teacher Khalistha has been great help in my child’s Sec 2 science. The lesson is clear and many worksheets was given out during lesson for my child to practice. Difficult concepts are explained in a simple way, which make it easier to understand and apply. have seem some improvement in both confidence and results. Would highly recommend Edufirst.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Keat Hong) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: I like that they are very flexible in the tuition timings and dates.  You won't have problem finding another make up time for missed lesson.  Only request is if the tutor and provide maybe monthly feedback so I would know whether he is coping well or not.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Keat Hong) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: My experience with EduFirst @ Keat Hong has been positive thus far. Their communication is great - updates and responses are prompt, if not fast, which gives us great peace of mind. They allow parents to pick up children until 8pm, which is a real saving grace when I need to work late. They also cater for special dietary requirements. I really appreciate these added consideration and flexibility. My child is happy and I feel assured.

place_name: EduFirst Learning Centre (Keat Hong) - English Math Chinese Science Tuition Centre Singapore
review_text: My child is currently attending this student care and no regrets at all! The teachers are very caring and prompt at updating matters involving child. And the place is also clean and well kept. It is also placed in a convenient location for us staying around the area. Highly recommended!

Keat Hong LRT Station (BP3) is a light rail station along the Bukit Panjang LRT (BPLRT). Located within the Choa Chu Kang estate, the station is overground and located at the junction of Choa Chu Kang Way and Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1.

Taking its name from a former army camp in the vicinity (Keat Hong Camp), the station largely serves residential estates in the vicinity.

LRT station BP3 from Bukit Panjang MRT LRT conveniently located at Choa Chu Kang Way next to Keat Hong Mirage complex with popular Hey Sugar icecream Shop walking distance to Palm Gardens Condominium Keat Hong Quad Housing Complex Keat Hong Garden Park with children's playground jogging tracks sports facilities shade Pavilion strolling trails amphitheatres and breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Few LRT stops via South View LRT to Choa Chu Kang MRT LRT Lot One Mall with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries Electric Electronic telecommunication postage services household products community centre and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Train delayed second time within 2 weeks time. Announcement totally not clear, no display in text on the delay and the reason. No announce on website on the delay either. Hard to imagine that this kind of level of service happen in developed country like singapore.
Clean and peaceful location. There are two platforms. When you go to the platform make sure you are in correct platform by checking the train direction sign boad to confirm your destination.

Opened on 6 November 1999 as part of the Bukit Panjang LRT line operated by SMRT Trains, this LRT station is located at Choa Chu Kang Way near the junction with Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Choa Chu Kang Neighbourhoods 1, 2 and 8 along Choa Chu Kang Central, Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1 and Teck Whye Lane along with the residents of Palm Gardens Condominium at Hong San Walk bringing them along to Teck Whye Garden, Teck Whye Shopping Centre as well as Choa Chu Kang Town Centre (Lot One, Choa Chu Kang Centre, Choa Chu Kang MRT/LRT Station and Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange). The bus services serving Keat Hong LRT station are SMRT Buses service 67 between Choa Chu Kang and Tampines, SMRT Buses service 188 and Express service 188e between Choa Chu Kang and HarbourFront, SBS Transit service 974 between Joo Koon and Bukit Panjang MRT/LRT Station, SMRT Buses service 976 between Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange and Bukit Panjang Bus Interchange and SMRT Buses Express service 982E between Choa Chu Kang Avenue 5 and Marina Boulevard. Keat Hong LRT station is named after the former army camp in the vicinity called Keat Hong Camp which was located at 261 Choa Chu Kang Avenue 1 before being relocated to 611 Old Choa Chu Kang Road as the land where the former army camp was located at was occupied by the Keat Hong Garden as well as the Keat Hong Axis, Keat Hong Quad and Keat Hong Mirage HDB flats.

Happened to come across this home-based cafe on google maps and decided to give it a try.

I got the vanilla latte & maple cinnamon latte and was super happy with both drinks. Particularly enjoyed the maple cinnamon one as it was a good drink combo.

Prices were quite affordable and found it easy to pre-order the drinks. They were really friendly and responsive as well.

Will definitely be coming back for more :)

Chanced upon this home cafe while searching for coffee and decided to give it a try — so glad I did. Made an order and it came in these really cute little bags, which was such a nice touch. I tried the hazelnut coffee and it was nicely roasted and perfectly sweetened, very easy to enjoy. Love that they also do festive gift packaging — makes it a great option for gifting to friends. Will definitely order again!

Ordered their Maple Cinnamon oat milk latte and thought it was quite a pleasant tasting drink. A bit on the sweet side, but you can request for less sweet. I like how it's one of the few cafes with Oat milk as the base!

Appreciated how the coffee was nutty and creamy with a good balance between the espresso and milk.  Had the iced oat latte. Owner was also friendly and the process of ordering from them was smooth! Would definitely order again!
Been getting this coffee for a while now. Love the taste of the coffee..definitely will keep ordering in future!

This home-based coffee place makes very decent coffee! The menu has quite a wide selection, including food items; and the takeaway bag is a nice touch.

Only thing to note is that this is more suited as a grab-and-go spot.
Just had an iced latte from The Cozy Café and it was sooo good. Smooth, creamy, and super refreshing — not too sweet, not too bitter. Perfect little pick-me-up. Will definitely be back!

Placed my order via Grab. Pretty cute packaging and  logo design 😊 I went to pick it up, and apologies for ringing the doorbell as I didn't realized my drinks are placed at a dedicated area. I will order again!

Searched for coffee on grab and saw that the menu here was quite interesting so decided to give it a try! It was very unassumingly good! The pistachio oat milk latte was so nice! The pistachio, coffee and milk balanced each other nicely. Much nicer than any pistachio latte I’ve tried before!

Thought it was a small cafe that we have not heard of, didn’t know it was a home based business! Definitely will order again!

The drinks are decent enough. I had the iced matcha, which was fine. The bagels, however, are a different story. The chocolate lava bagel was rock-hard and honestly didn’t taste great either. Stick to the drinks and skip the food.

place_name: Hong Kiat Seafood Restaurant (豐吉海鲜馆)
review_text: Finally get to try this after running pass this area so many times. Food is good and service fast.

place_name: Hong Kiat Seafood Restaurant (豐吉海鲜馆)
review_text: Old school Zi Chai which is still good and tasty. Prices has creep up over the years. $32 for 1 rice, 1 meat and 1 veg for 2pax but if the taste is good is worth the price. We finish every single mossel

review_text: Used to be a regular here until we moved out of the area, during the good old days cars were parked along the road. Glad to see that the food quality maintained, service is (lightning) fast and friendly. I love their Kam Heong fish head, has to be one of the best in Singapore.

place_name: Hong Kiat Seafood Restaurant (豐吉海鲜馆)
review_text:
$89 for 4 dishes and 3 rice.

Curry fish head: spicy and sour.

Salted egg prawns: my sister like it

place_name: Hong Kiat Seafood Restaurant (豐吉海鲜馆)
review_text:
The last time dining in was about 4 - 5 years ago, we make  a special visit to try again.
We have ordered the black pepper deer meat, house special cooked sotong, prawn omelette and housemade beancurd.
First tried and found their standard doesn't improve... deer meat is too much water and doesn't bring up black pepper spice. Sotong is very normal and doesn't tasted any special, omelette just ok and not salty and similar to beancurd no special too, perhaps can able to find from others stalls in Singapore. I have noticed many ordered their fishhead curry.  Maybe next time will try (if)

A classic heartland neighbourhood mall serving the daily needs of nearby residents. The Shopping Centre has a good mix of food options, including a renovated food centre, coffee shops & small eateries, along with essential services like supermarkets, clinics & hair salons.

The vibe is very local & old-school — practical with a strong community feel. It’s convenient, especially for those living nearby.
Only managed to try the yong tau foo and braised duck. Seems like many of the original stores are gone. Quite windy but still hot in this June period.

This is truly as local a shopping centre as you can find. Within a small two storey building, there's a prime supermarket, a grocery store, several coffee shops, and stalls selling fruits, electronic gadgets, etc.
Just behind is a wet market, and even a small temple. Oh, I also spotted a KFC. So yeah, if you're in the area, you can drop by for some fried chicken and soya bean.

Think this is a newly opened stall at L1 of this neighbourhood shopping centre. Good price and good food. Nothing more to ask for except maybe more seats which probably restricted by shop size. Would say a hidden gem in the west. Will be back to try other dishes.

as a local living here, it's in need of upgrading. i have no idea why the place has not been at all upgraded. it's fairly small, pretty dirty (there's cock roaches). if it got upgraded im sure more people would come here.

Convenient. Upgraded to cleaner look and better organised. Carries more chinese snacks now. But be careful when buying fruits and vegetables as there's mould sometimes. I once bought an entire carton of mandarin oranges which turned out to be all mouldy. They do not allow cash refund but equivalent value exchange. Sometimes promotion items are reflected as original price when checking out, need to check the receipt carefully.
Needs more Aircon inside. Very hot . Bcos they have 2 big open entrance n can't feel any air-conditioning.But the seafood section is the best compare to others supermarket.

Very disappointing shopping experience. The price was clearly displayed in front of the UFC coconut water, and my husband and I double-checked it. However, at the payment counter, the price charged was higher than the listed price.

When I asked the counter staff, he did not check anything, or double confirm the price of the item, and simply told us that they just follow the serial number, which felt like he was just brushing us off. I am very sure the displayed price corresponded to the correct item.

What disappointed me most was the staff’s attitude and the fact that incorrect prices are frequently displayed. This has happened more than once, and the staff was really impatient. I don’t known how’s the boss’s management, what I see is that employees are not very responsible in carrying out their duties.
Suggest improving the payment speed. While we understand and support hiring elderly staff, enabling auto-payment could really help. Waiting 20 minutes just to buy something is a bit too long.

Coffee I bought last week appeared to be mouldy and inside is hardened. And why are you still selling sprouted potatoes? Please focus on selling edible items instead of wasting time and money renovating.

I had an unpleasant experience at this supermarket regarding pricing accuracy. (Not first time encountered)

The shelf price displayed for a product was different from the price charged at the cashier. As a customer, I expect the price shown on the shelf to match the amount charged at checkout.

After noticing the discrepancy, I had to spend additional time clarifying the issue. Accurate pricing is a basic expectation, and such discrepancies can be misleading and inconvenient for customers.

I hope the management will improve its price-tag maintenance and conduct regular checks to ensure that shelf prices are updated promptly and accurately.

Please remember to verify your receipt after payment.

Please be responsible and accountable.
Really hope other supermarket can take over!

A well-stocked minimart with most of the daily supplies you will need.
Poor service of staff.

This gym is easily the best in the area. The facility is always spotless, the equipment is top-tier, and there’s a great variety of machines so I never have to wait. What really sets it apart, though, is the community atmosphere—the staff is incredibly welcoming and genuinely invested in your progress. Highly recommend to anyone from beginners to pros!

My safe place for the past year! Very clean, very nice and attentive staff, the equipments are up-to date and the showers are also neat and tidy! Thank you Uncle Hassan and Hafa for making this gym feel like home!

Great experience at 24/7 Fitness Keat Hong CC. Range of equipments like Gym80, LifeFitness and Hammer Strength. Personal favourites will be the Cybex Hack Squat and the Gym80 Pendulum Squat. Really recommend 👍. The owners Hassan & Hafa were also very helpful and friendly.

Amazing 24/7 place to workout in. The gym is always kept clean, in part due to the hardworking staff but also due to the awesome community GYMMBOXX Keat Hong has built. A senior staff, Mr Hassan, is a warm face you can expect to see during its staffed hours. The trainer is a loud and friendly presence on the floor, his voice not only encourages the trainee but those around him to push harder. My experience, in the many years I have worked out in this outlet, has been nothing short of greatness. Would recommend to others to check it out!

The staff are friendly and welcoming, which really adds to the overall experience. Their positive attitude creates a warm, comfortable atmosphere that makes the gym a pleasant place to work out. You can tell they care about making everyone feel at ease and supported.

Thank you for taking over Cathay! I really enjoyed the Premiure Class experience. Food ordering was easy and steamless. The seat are spacious and comfortable. Thank you for taking over Cathay — the Premiere Class experience at Shaw Theatres was truly impressive 👏

From the moment we stepped in, everything felt elevated. The ordering process was seamless and fuss-free, making the whole experience feel smooth and well thought out.

The seats were incredibly spacious and plush, perfect for fully relaxing throughout the movie. What really stood out was the thick, cozy blanket — a small touch, but it made a huge difference in comfort and warmth.

Overall, it’s a premium cinema experience that delivers on both comfort and convenience. Definitely worth it for a more luxurious movie night — will be back again! 🎬✨

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

Very clean and movie quality very clear and good!👍🏻✨

Overall very nice, staffs also very friendly. Bought tickets through machine easy to use.
👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨✨👍🏻✨👍🏻👍🏻✨

my first time at shaw theatres jem! very clean and the seats were really comfortable
i was surprised by the sound system here, it’s really really good, even for a normal digital screening! out of the three times i’ve watch project hail mary this was by far the best experience
5 stars 🤩

first time coming to jem for a premium movie and it was a good experience. we only got fries but i was surprised they had ice creams and alcohol available too. the whole atmosphere is calm with dim lighting, plush seats and tons of legroom. it’s super family friendly but still feels grown up so you can actually relax. kamalii gave us a quick tour and explained all about the premium options so we knew what we were getting. the premium waiting area looks excellent and the service was really good, way better than the other outlets i’ve been to. she made us feel like vips, not just another booking. we’re definitely coming back here soon.

A combine public space with Playgrounds for all ages, Street soccer court, basketball court and running track. No washroom or drinking water dispenser in vicinity though sadly, also not much shady spots too. else all is good for family time.

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

review_text:
Since 2013, we have entrusted Mr. Mike and Ms. Kit with tutoring our eldest daughter. Impressed by their dedication and the results achieved, we decided to enroll our other three children with them as well, taking various subjects over the years.

From Primary School Math and Science to Secondary level E-Math, A-Math, and Chemistry, their teaching methods have helped our children gain a clearer understanding of the subjects and apply what they've learned effectively in school.

They go the extra mile to understand what topics will be tested and ensure targeted revision is done in preparation for exams. Sending our children to Top Grades Tuition Centre has truly been a worthwhile investment in their educational journey.

place_name: Top Grades Tuition & Enrichment @ Yew Tee, Choa Chu Kang - Visit our IG for Genuine Feedback fr Parents & Students
review_text:
I had a truly life-changing experience with Mike as my tutor. Back when I was in Secondary 3, I was struggling badly with Additional Mathematics and consistently failing. My school even gave me an ultimatum — either pass the next exam or be forced to drop the subject.

Feeling lost, I turned to Mike for help. Although he initially planned to start his classes at the beginning of the school year, he made a special exception for me and even started earlier — I was his very first student. That level of dedication already spoke volumes.

Under his guidance, everything began to click. He was patient, clear in his explanations, and genuinely invested in my progress. With his help, I not only passed the exam but did so with flying colours. More importantly, I was able to continue improving and perform well in Additional Mathematics for the rest of my secondary school years.

I’m incredibly grateful for his support and commitment. I would highly recommend Mike to anyone who needs help — especially if you’re struggling and unsure where to start.

Pretty small compared to the other public libraries but cozy and clean. Shelves and the area in general are well maintained too. There are 2 sides of the library - one for the children and the other for adults. Good thinking with the placement of many seats on both ends. In terms of crowd, I’d say this one is slightly less crowded than other branches as well.

My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I have never seen a library like Bukit Panjang. It’s in a mall, and once you go to the highest level of the mall, you’ll notice there are 2 Bukit Panjang Libraries, when in reality they only have one, just the kind of spread it out a bit. On one side of the level, there’s a children’s section. On the other side, there’s an adults & teens zone.

The children’s zone is very spacious. The design of the children’s zone is very different and kind of unique. In the middle of the room, there’s these bookshelves on this circle thing, and you can walk up there, and there are a few shelves there too. They also have these like walls that are kind of separated, they form a circle, and you can sit in between the walls.

In the adult & teens zone, one of the first things I noticed was the shelves for the magazines. They were almost like a zig-zag in a sense. When you first walk in, you can see this swirly thing where they have a few books on display.

When you look up, you can notice the design close to the ceiling, and I think it’s really cool. Like the children’s zone, they also have separated walls that form a bit of a circle, and you can sit there, and work on your computers, study, and read. They also have audiovisuals, and a section with tables and computers.

There’s also this circle thing, once you’ve walked in a bit farther and turned to the right. It’s filled with bookshelves, tables, seats, and people. I noticed everyone that was sitting there was studying on their computers. Which makes me think the Bukit Panjang Library is a really nice place to study at. But I think it would be a bit harder to get a seat if you don’t come early.

Overall, I personally really like this library because I think you can do diverse things there like, reading, listening to an audiobook, working on your computers, studying, and more.
It’s small but very well maintained; bright and clean, with books of 4 main languages in Singapore. The staff are friendly and helpful in this library.
Nice and cosy with adult and children library separated at different corners but on the same level of shopping mall.

After this library was revonated the books that was added was quite a lot and I like how they separate the children and adults sections for the peace with the adults and the usual fun noise children make this library is also very spacious with quite a lot of cubicles to sit on

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve is one of the best places in Singapore to experience nature in its most authentic form. From the moment I entered via the Neo Tiew Crescent entrance, I was surrounded by lush greenery, peaceful trails, and the soothing sounds of wildlife

The reserve is exceptionally well-maintained, with clear signage, wide boardwalks, and excellent visitor facilities at the Wetland Centre. The elevated wooden walkways make it easy to explore the mangrove forests while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding wetlands

One of the highlights of my visit was observing mudskippers hopping across the mudflats among the mangrove roots. It was fascinating to see these unique creatures thriving in their natural habitat. The reserve offers plenty of opportunities to spot wildlife, so keep an eye out for monitor lizards, crabs, and migratory birds

I was also impressed by the Mudskipper Pod, a beautifully designed wooden observation structure that blends seamlessly into the environment. It provides a cool and shaded resting point with panoramic views over the mangroves and the Johor Strait. The architecture adds a unique touch to the overall experience without detracting from the natural surroundings

A refreshing escape into nature! We enjoyed a slow and peaceful walk at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve on May 27, fully immersed in the calm beauty of the wetlands. The walking paths were beautifully lined with lush trees, creating a naturally shaded and cooling environment that made the stroll especially comfortable even during the warmer hours.
The tranquil surroundings, gentle sounds of nature, and fresh air provided a relaxing break from the busy city atmosphere. Along the way, we appreciated the well-maintained trails and the opportunity to observe the rich biodiversity and greenery up close.
It is a wonderful place for a leisurely walk, nature appreciation, photography, or simply to unwind and recharge. Highly recommended for anyone looking to enjoy a peaceful and scenic outdoor experience in Singapore.

A beautiful and peaceful escape into nature! The mangroves, fresh air, and rich wildlife make every walk feel special. Perfect for a relaxing stroll or bird watching—especially in the early morning or evening.
Stay curious and keep your eyes open… you might spot a giant monitor lizard crossing the path or even a crocodile resting in the water. A little adventure awaits you here!

My family took a late afternoon walk down to Sungei Buloh during low tide today to unwind, and it was a great way to clear our heads. The air had that familiar, heavy scent of salt and damp mangrove mud, and with the water stripped back, the exposed api-api roots and glistening mudflats completely changed the landscape. We spent a while just watching the giant mudskippers drag themselves across the silt and bicker over territory, which is always entertaining. The low water also meant the local birds were out in full force. We managed to spot a Great Egret freezing in place before spearing a small fish, and a Grey Heron standing completely still in the shallows waiting for Archerfish. Finishing up on the main bridge during the golden hour was the highlight, looking down into the shallow river channel at schools of Halfbeaks skimming the surface under the evening sky was incredibly peaceful and a solid reminder of how nice it is to have this kind of wilderness right on our doorstep.
Really nice nature walk. There is one bus that goes there. Can spend about 3-4 hours there. You get to see the swamps, facing JB, lizards, fishes, crabs, birds etc.

Pang Sua Pond is a peaceful and calm place to relax and unwind. There’s a large pond, a walking bridge, and a nicely maintained landscape area. You can also enjoy a beautiful panoramic view of the nearby town. I spent about 2–3 hours here with my family and really enjoyed the quiet atmosphere. Highly recommended if you're nearby and looking for a relaxing spot.

1. Early in the 1990s, it was a stormwater collection ponds built to combat the flooding during heavy downpour by collecting surface runoff.
2. The renovation was completed on 25/3/2017.
3. In SG, the Pang Sua Pond Floating Wetland is one of the largest man-made floating wetlands with leather fern, water canna, and the miniature flatsedge.
4. The bridge inside is a 480-meter long.
5. Area of constructed floating wetlands 2,000m²; Area of Pang Sua Pond 3.2 hectares (32,000m²)

Pang Sua Pond is an absolute 5-star gem and a testament to what a beautifully designed public space can be. This isn't just a pond; it's a stunning stormwater collection point (part of the ABC Waters project) that has been transformed into a vibrant community hub.

The main feature is the wonderful boardwalk that stretches across and around the water, connecting the different parts of the neighbourhood. It's my go-to spot for a peaceful morning walk or a relaxing stroll in the evening. As my photos show, the water is often incredibly still, creating stunning, calm reflections of the surrounding HDBs and the sky.

It's also a fantastic spot for nature lovers. It's teeming with wildlife! You can often see herons and egrets, and I've frequently spotted large monitor lizards (like the one in my video) swimming calmly in the water, which is always a treat to see.

One of the best practical things about this pond is its location. It's conveniently surrounded by quite a number of food courts and kopitiams. This makes it the perfect place for a relaxing stroll after a heavy meal from one of the nearby eateries, helping to walk off all those extra calories.

It's a beautiful, tranquil, and practical public space that perfectly blends nature with heartland living. Highly recommended for residents and visitors alike!

It's a hidden gem in a busy neighbourhood. I love taking an evening stroll around the pond, listening to the sounds of nature, watching the fish swim, elders exercising and people enjoying leisure walk. It's a peaceful and scenic spot beside Senja-Cashew Community Club near Bukit Panjang MRT.

Great view and excellent for chilling especially with friends and in the evening, the environment is very nice. There were also a lot of fishes and resting places. Overall, highly recommended for those who want to relax in the evening.

Some landlord had converted their place from 3 rooms to 6 rooms and offer short term lease and their tenants do not abide by the bye laws and were smoking at the stairwell. Beside being a fire hazard, there was second hand smoke and littering(cigarette butts and phlegm can be seen). Dog poops seen on the common walkway, lifts breakdown frequently due to heavy usage, access doors and intercom are not working too. Cleaners used to mop the common area twice a day but now I do not see them around and stains can be found and not clean for weeks. Even witnessed kpod/vape users in the estate, reported to security guards who ignored this issue saying they have no evidence. The boys were not even walking properly and both fell down together. This place is slowly becoming a slum.
Nice condominium with banks, food courts and MRT and bus interchange nearby.

Well maintained condominium with good accessibility. The condo has a small gym, function rooms, tennis court, BBQ pits, and 3 pools- Lap pool, Main pool and children pool and also Jacuzzis. Mrt, bus interchange and Lot1 mall at walkable distance ( Just on the opposite side of the road). Choachukang Park is also at a walkable distance which has kids play area, toddlers play area, 24hours food center- Teh Tarik, Therapeutic garden and Community garden. There is also an Indian Grocery store, 24 hour food centre and Food fare and Keat Hong CC at a walkable distance.

Calm quiet and well kept. Really near to the MRT and bus station. Pretty big main pool and then a smaller one, 3 Jacuzzis. Pretty well equipped gym. BBQ pits and then a play pool for kids. Jogging track is there as well.

I’ve stayed at The Warren for quite a while and it’s been a great experience. The facilities are well maintained — from the pool to the gym, everything is kept clean and in good working order. The location is incredibly convenient too. It's just a short walk to the bus interchange and CCK MRT, with the upcoming Jurong Region Line adding even more accessibility in the near future. It will also be just opposite the upcoming integrated bus interchange/food centre.

You’re also close to nature and leisure — Choa Chu Kang Park and SAFRA is just around the corner if you’re into jogging, sports or weekend activities. With shopping, food options, and daily essentials all within easy reach, The Warren really strikes a great balance between convenience and comfortable living.

Highly recommend for anyone looking for a well-connected home in the west!

We enrolled our son at Heartyland Infant Care (Teck Whye) when he was just 3 months old. Being a Dragon year baby, it was highly competitive to find infant care and we’re so grateful we got a spot here.

Their love and dedication truly stand out, they have hearts of gold.

As first-time parents, we’ve also learned so much through their advice and support. A special shoutout to Mrs. June Lee and Teacher Faridah — your care, patience, and warmth have made such a difference in our journey.

Our son enjoys his time at Heartyland, and even though the facilities are slightly older, what truly matters is the people and the teachers here are amazing.

Highly recommended to any parent looking for a nurturing and supportive infant care centre!

This is pertaining to Heartyland Childcare at Mayspring Condo. The principal Mrs Lee is very money minded and refused to return the deposit of 150 dollars  we paid after we decided not to enrol in her centre. She even said that if next time we want to enrol again on the centre, we need to repay the deposit.

Also , was appalled by the way she handle kids behaviour.. Had mentioned to her my child was pushed down by another child in an indoor playground and I’m quite upset by the other boy’s behaviour.  She retorted my child should defend himself by either pushing the child back or avoid the child by running away. Was quite shocked she suggested defending oneself by getting even with the other child. It seems pretty unkind of her to make such suggestion.

Updated review: Regarding the reply, building resilience does not mean retaliating by pushing another child back and hurting the child.  And btw, ur the one who keep pushing us to  secure a slot.You collected money from us very swiftly and  did not even ask us to consider. It leads me thinking, with such a vindictive principal will my child be able to cultivate good moral values or be safe in that centre?!

The best IFC a parent & child could ever ask for! My LO is always looking forward to go to school every single day. If you expect perfection, I'd suggest you take care of your child yourself. Would definitely recommend anyone whose living around the area!

Trustworthy caring nurturing and professional.

Love how much they care for every child there.
Best childcare for your child

place_name: Graceland Student Care Centre @ Teck Whye

review_text: I have both my children in Graceland since they are in Primary 1. Appreciate that the teachers have ensured that their schoolwork are completed on time so that I don't have to worry about at night.
