place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - CCK & Bukit Panjang
review_text: I am very thankful to the teacher Mani at Five Star tuition for supported my child in preparing for the PSLE Science exam. The lessons were clear, engaging, and helped my child understand the concepts much better. The teacher was very patient and encouraging, which greatly improved my child’s confidence in science and achieved good results. Highly recommended for students preparing for PSLE

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - CCK & Bukit Panjang
review_text:
My daughter struggled with English and Science in Primary 4, so we decided to enroll her in Five Star Tuition to prepare for the PSLE after seeing their positive Google reviews. It was one of the best choices we ever made. She got a AL1 for her science and overall great result ending up in her first choice of secondary school !

She gained a much better understanding of Science topics because the teacher explained concepts so clearly. The notes and practice materials provided by the center were excellent and made her revision much more manageable. Thanks to the support from Five Star and her own hard work, she aced her PSLE and got into her first-choice secondary school! I highly recommend Five Star to any parent looking to give their child the extra support they need to excel !

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - CCK & Bukit Panjang
review_text: I am an ex student of this tution centre from the batch of 2018. Currently i am studying in Nanyang Technological University (NTU). Five star tuition centre has been a big my pillar in this journey of mine and has helped me ace through various important examinations, especially PSLE. All of their resources are really useful and are constantly updated according to the current trend of questions. In my PSLE paper, there many questions with their model answers and all their practice oral topics also came in as a big boost. Overall, the efficient, resilient and in-depth teaching offered in this tiition centre is really helpful as they follow the method of smart work rather than hard work. This tution centre is always close my heart, and i highly recommend this centre for immediate improvement!!!

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - CCK & Bukit Panjang
review_text:
Best materials for my 4 kids since year 2017 to 2024. All my 4 kids graduated from FIVE STAR CENTRE © well settled now in best secondary schools and top universities - NUS, making parents proud of their achievement...

FIVE STAR Tuition is really 5 STAR rating so parents can send their kids there without worry and be prepared to receive many many materials and notes for their P6 entire year and need to work hard for their workshops and weekly homework. They will also give feedbacks if your kids do not do/bring their homework - That's the best part for PARENTS like us!
All the best FIVE STAR TUITION...

place_name: Five Star Tuition Centre Pte Ltd - CCK & Bukit Panjang
review_text: I was struggling to pass mathematics and Science, and I consecutively failed maths and Science for 4 years in primary school but after I joined this tution I saw a lot of improvement. There was also free workshops for mathematics, science and English which helped me so much and I also gained a lot of confidence. Furthermore they also did a lot of fun of experiment and learning journeys which i benefited from. So I’m really glad that I joined this tution and I would strongly recommend other students to join as well.

Opened on 6 November 1999 as part of the Bukit Panjang LRT line operated by SMRT Trains, this LRT station is located at Choa Chu Kang Way near the junction with Choa Chu Kang Road and Bukit Batok Road. The station brings direct rail connectivity for residents of Teck Whye Heights along Teck Whye Lane along with the residents of Sol Acres Condominium at Choa Chu Kang Grove and students of Institute of Technical Education (ITE) College West bringing them along to Bukit Gombak Park, Pavilion Park, Teck Whye Shopping Centre as well as Choa Chu Kang Town Centre (Lot One, Choa Chu Kang Centre, Choa Chu Kang MRT/LRT Station and Choa Chu Kang Bus Interchange). The bus services serving Teck Whye LRT station are SMRT Buses service 67, 180, 180A, 187, 976, express 982E, 983, 983A and 985 as well as SBS Transit bus services 160, 974 and 974A and the LRT station is named after the nearby Teck Whye Heights housing estate which is located at Teck Whye Lane.

Nearest LRT station to Institute of Technical Education College West and the popular Phoenix Heights Playground Bukit Batok Park and walking distance to Bukit Batok Town Park with Bukit Gombak Stadium Little Guilin Lakeview and breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes. Two LRT stops to BP6 Bukit Panjang Bukit Panjang Library Senja Cashew Community Club with scenic Pang Sua Pond Hillion Mall Bukit Panjang Plaza with plenty of dinning shopping banking supermarkets library cinemas entertainments groceries sundries Electric Electronic postage services community centre and amenities nearby.
Walking distance to Choa Chu Kang Community Club Teck Whye Shopping Centre Fairprice Teck Whye Lane Sheng Siong Supermarket Sinaran Seafood Restaurant Rasa Rasa Restaurant Markozar Desert Amber @ West Restaurant and Matrix @ ITE college. Freshly prepared food with healthy ingredients tender loving skillful cooking and warm services. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

Nothing to rate, a LRT stop with a convenience store manned by an elderly lady selling tidbits and drinks. 30m away the nearest block to the station there is a mama shop that sells more variety of things.
I like it, but I couldn't find a toilet there, but I'm not sure if there was a toilet there.
Air conditioning in the LRT is horrible it’s either freezing cold or very hot inside
Didn't went there but OK
Been there for 5 days, very clam and neat station, likes to travel the place again
Clean and never crowded. The little shop at the tap-out area opens infrequently, and sells dated items. Wheelchair-friendly station; has a lift.
Teck whye
Convenient LRT for the ITE students here
weird bus stop that only has the bus stop pole and only serves as a terminating point for 983M. No boarding allowed for now. So unique and special
The best neighbourhood and plenty of coffeshops to choose from, unfortunately, very limited seating capacity during the peak hours. Value for money on food selection.

Love coming here in the morning after 8 am. for the Nam Kee " Mee Hoon Kueh." The vegetarian bee hoon at B1 with curry is also delicious. Starbucks is always quiet here. Nice to just buy a coffee and sit to think 🤔  whatever you desire. Come, you will be spoiled for choice what to eat.

Classic old school "shopping mall". Like back to the past. Unfortunately for now still packed with residents though it's Covid period. Otherwise love this place .... Only thing ...hardly can find seats to makan
An interesting place with a good mix of shops to serve the daily needs of people living there.

One can find literally everything here for your home and to fill your hunger
It's shoulder to shoulder kinda of crowd on Sundays

Portion is huge, ordered xiao wan mian at only $5. I personally don’t like the noodles used here. Has got the weird taste. Soup is okay, many ingredients used, even has got wanton inside.

Long queue, and hot environment. May want to try their laksa the next time.

At $4 a bowl, the quantity is huge. To me it's a hybrid between laksa n curry mee. For laksa, it lacks the depth n should have more dried shrimp. As for curry mee, it is too milky n thick. However, it would be nice to add some steam chicken to enhance the flavor as curry mee.
For the price n quantity, no one should complain.

Good neighborhood laksa . No frills and wholesome . Thick gravy and flavorful!
Will try their Lor Mee and fish ball noodles next visit .

Just a small coffee shop with one drinks stall, one noodle stall and one vegetarian stall.

Noodle stall is quite popular selling Fishball Noodle, laksa, lor mee, little bowl noodle and fish soup. Make payment at the drinks stall first before ordering at the noodle stall itself.

Vegetarian stall have vegetarian bee hoon and daily specials.

Both noodle and vegetarian stalls operate till after lunch hour.

Coffee stall open till around evening time.

Very nice Lor Mee in CCK area. The braised pork belly is very fragrant and tender. The portion of the sliced ngor hiang given was quite big. To me, the most important part of Lor Mee is the Lor. The Lor is very thick and fragrant. I was abit skeptical at first because it looks lighter in colour compared to others. But I was wrong, it's highly fragrant esp after they put the garlic and black vinegar!

In my opinion, they win Tian Fa noodle house hands down.

Amazing experience. The variety of dishes, such as fish, black pepper beef, and lemon fried chicken were delicious.  Service was top-notch. Very attentive staff. Portions are generous, so you definitely won't leave hungry. Highly recommended.

A legendary spot that’s been around for years. The taste is consistent and averagely good, nothing much has changed, including the place itself. A little renovation could make it more exciting.

Tried the Nasi Goreng Thai (strong turmeric taste), Char Kway Teow (nice), and the Sotong Goreng Tepung, which was the best—soft and not chewy. Presentation is basic but the food does the job.
Nice food over here. Next door got one western food also very nice.

The price is reasonable but some foods alitle salty. The fried frice was not to my taste as they anyhow cooked. Only live the black pepper beef. Serving fast. Only can pay by paynow, cash & cdc vouchers.

When you are hungry and really can't decide what to eat, the decision and choice is simple, Chicken Rice lah 😋

Sinaran has always been our families favorite place to gather and enjoy a meal and that means 2 tables of 10 or 3 tables of 8. Most of the time the food is pretty consistent and delish. Out of the numerous times, only twice did the meal was sort of sub par, of what is expected. I guess that was a fluke. But needs to be said, 99% they are awesome 👌

This particular time, since we are not able to sit and enjoy our meal, we decided to just pack and go. As usual, the chicken rice is almost perfect. Moist and juicy chicken meat, rice that is not oily and yet so flavourful and portioning that is very generous. Additionally, got the rojak too. Not disappointing for sure 😋

My only gripe of late would be how they manage orders. Really could and must do better. They MUST adhere to the "first come first serve" rule,  PERIOD. It is not fair for patrons who come earlier having to wait while others who come after gets their food first. However we try to be understanding, it really does and will get to anyone seeing this happening. So for now until they better the ordering process, then it's just 3 stars.

Service and food quality is equally important.

Food : 4 / 5
Staff : 3 / 5
Ambience: 3 / 5

Very disappointed with both dishes. I ordered via GrabFood and it was quite expensive for what I received. The Hokkien mee was bland, watery, and tasted nothing like proper Hokkien mee, with barely any ingredients—just a small prawn and a few pieces of squid.

The crispy noodle was just as bad—mostly plain noodles with very little flavor, hardly any vegetables, only a small slice of fish cake and what tasted like frozen squid. Overall, poor quality and not worth the price. Would not recommend at all 👎

Had a satisfying bowl of roasted chicken laksa on a pouring CNY evening in Teck Whye's Rasa Rasa 🍜

The spice level was just right - rich, flavourful, not overpowering.. There’s the options of chicken or seafood. This outlet is especially generous with their portions, plenty of meat & a hearty amount of broth for $5.50

Special shoutout to Chef Akash, who is always very warm, polite & friendly. Highly recommend the Humfull Laksa here, coffee is pretty good too 👍🏼

Eat in here on a Saturday night til Sunday early wee morning.  Had the tarik, kopi O, Nescafé. Place is open 24 hours. And carpark is the multi storey carpark.  Ordered grab food from here before too several days be it during morning/afternoon hours. Had their laksa, porridge before. Markozar banana fritters is good too~ overall convenient place for small group 2-3 pax up to bigger groups, 8-10 pax or more even. 👍

Standards have severely dropped. I ordered Chendol as my drink which was a disaster it tasted bitter rather than sweet.

Next up the mains: Nasi Goreng Campung & Sambal Nasi Goreng
The rice was average compared to the prime days. The chicken chunks were also lesser for the rice quantity they gave

Overall, an underwhelming experience with them who’s has been a regular customer for years, I hope they can bounce back and regain their prime days’ standards. Cheers!☺️

Tasted their Sambal Fried Rice, Pattaya Fried Rice, Mee Goreng Basah, Tom Yum Fried Rice, Beef and Chicken Fried Rice. Please stop using the Fried Rice “sambal” in every of your normal fried dishes. Even when requesting less spicy, it still turns out very spicy. Not everyone can tolerate the heat and you can even feel the burning sensation on your lips. It would be appreciated if the kitchen could adjust the spice level properly. Btw, the Pattaya Fried Rice in the photo, is it Pattaya Sambal Fried Rice or just the Pattaya Fried Rice that I ordered??

4.40 for 1 veg 1 meat?
This price is going way beyond control. The mixed rice shop has inconsistent pricing and calculate based on their mood. I come here and eat everyday and this is just too much. If this is not handled, i will lodge an official consumer report.

Nice & Delicious Vegetarian Sweet & Sour Pork Rice! 🍛😋. (Stall : Red Apple Vegetarian)

Its pork is crispy & yet soft paired with a balanced sweet sour sauce and freshly cut 🥒+🍍+🫑+🥬. It's been a while since I've come across such a Vegetarian stall. Highly recommended!

Price wise a bit high side but still decent & is quite filling though.

Remember to order the Kopi-C siu dai, it's coffee really good. The right balance and intoxicating ☕ aroma 🤤

**
1. Came back again for Hor Fun this time. Not so bad but still got room for improvement ( Not piping hot as the sauce just drenched over it w/o cooking together with the Hor Fun, hence the texture not soft  & smooth enough to complement. )
Had dinner, delicious food and drinks served fast! Wasn’t crowded and cooling night. Photos were after taking half the food cause we’re too hungry 🥹 Happy Dinner 🤤
The 排骨王 was tender but slightly too sweet. The butter sotong and 宫保鸡丁 tasted nice but a bit too hard. The 油麦菜 lacked wok hei. Overall, average zi char stall for a simple dinner.

Update: The stall reopens again about a year after the old uncle retired! The black fried carrot cake tastes like old-time flavour with the right amount of wok hei. Char kway teow is the salty type, different from the usual sweet Singapore type, very authentic!

Chogan Char Kway Teow has an old-time flavour, dry style, less black soya source, done by a senior uncle. Hokkien fried prawn noodles and fried carrot cake from the same stall are really good too. Wanton noodles next stall is worth trying.

A sufficiently-stocked supermarket for your daily needs within the old Teck Whye housing estate.

Staff is friendly and for drivers, parking is very convenient with the surface car park just behind the supermarket.
Nice & quite to go shop there. Many items to choose. Thanka Sheng Siong.
Good to shop when hv promotions offer
The staff Sheng Siong, Teck Whye are friendly and helpful.  They make me feel comfortable, just like in the kampong spirit.

This marks my second visit to this outlet for crab, and once again, I was delighted to find their offerings exceptionally fresh, given their focus on live seafood. This time, we opted for a delectable butter seafood platter featuring crab, Lala, prawns, and an array of delightful side dishes. The entire family savored the meal with immense satisfaction, leaving us eagerly anticipating the next occasion we can indulge in this feast once more!

I went there after consultation at cck Polyclinic to buy 🫚 ginger; after selection I approached the chubby supervisor where can I have it weigh; he was busy talking to another Green Ts slim supervisor and found me annoying to ask him, he showed me face n use his finger to point inside mart. I walked to the right side after weigh scale,  to see if there is anyone else other than the bad svc chubby Supv could assist me, I found no one; only the chubby Supv was w/the slim Supv and a light grey thin uncle, at another end chatting again; so I walked all the way towards end of this mart n told them the problem I encountered n seemed again for help; the chubby Supv again not looking into eyes of customer, instead tell me to go to another unit if the mart whom I will get help from there. I visited Prime mart at NTU canteen 2, the service level of the staff there is friendly, helpful n approachable; unlike the svc level at Teck Whye that customers received.

I read here few customers feedback with 1-2 stars for Teck why outlet but there seems to be no reply from ops mgr, something very wrong? Dont you think so ? Mean prime mart doesn’t care at all for customers’ feedback ??

Worst Prime supermarket. Self checkout machines not in used. 2 cashiers manning the counter don't even know how to do their job. Only 1 person in queue and they took so long to rectify some cash payment issue. It's 2026, still need to employ people to do cashier job meh...🤦

They have a fair bit of variety, though. You can find food, fresh produce, homeware and stationery here. Cigarettes are also available.

Nice , have my fav goat milk

New, clean and food items are fresh. Shop assistants are friendly and helpful. Walking aisles are not too narrow. They've increased their variety over the years - now you can find homeware and books here.
Very small supermarket. Bought green apples from here and they were very fresh. I think the fresh meat section is quite limited

"She said the customer must asked lah" what if I purposely spend $25 so that I can avail of the promo then end up it's out of stock. That will be frustrating right? Staff

peak period and there is no counter open!!! what are your cashiers doing? just standing around

malay cashier liya zero vibe bipolar with regulars

Great service from the gym staff! Very friendly, approachable and always willing to help. Made me feel comfortable and welcomed. They were professional, attentive and patient in answering my questions. The environment felt welcoming and well-managed. Appreciate their support and positive attitude!

Inconsiderate gym user that just disregard the cleanliness for a shared facility and the rack is so close to the window, which leaves little space for manoeuvre after placing the safety mount. Please consider shifting the rack.

Had a pleasant time here with the staff during my sign up! The staff was very friendly and helpful with signing me up, there was many goodies like tshirt, shoe bag and a shaker bottle as well! Gym is nice and clean!

They’re just after your money. When you want to cancel your membership, they will give you the time and dates when there’ll will be a staff (Weekdays 12-8pm). However, when you come there, only an instructor who couldn’t accommodate you and will ask you to book an appointment. BS! They take your money on time but if you’re the one who needed them, they’re not available. 🤬
Excellent customer service from Ratna, she addressed all our concerns and queries in detail.... perfect balance of professionalism and friendliness

Best neighbourhood I've ever seen and went to. Well-maintained. Great playground place to play around. The playground was segregated to 2 different age group. Good surroundings. Great and Friendly people. There is a fitness corner too in the area in the middle of both playground. Good for everyone, 3 different generations could enjoy it here.
The best I've ever seen.

Really nice play area for both younger (2-5 years) and older (6-12 years) kids. The playground is segregated into two for the different age groups which we think it’s a great idea.

Theme of the playground is train for both, boys would love this playground!
So grateful that we have such playground here. Kids love it

It's a very well designed space!
There is an exercise area for elderly with wheelchairs, and workout equipment. Playground equipment for older children, and play area for young toddlers.
Carpark upstairs has a rooftop garden too if you are up to explore.
It is a very wholesome and inclusive 3 generation play area.
One of the better playgrounds ever built by the HDB next to an almost tranquil PCN path except for noise from the new BTO construction next to it.
New PG - install the obstacle courses rope web

Hindhede Nature Park is one of Singapore’s hidden gems for anyone looking to enjoy nature without committing to a long or strenuous hike. Despite its relatively small size, the park packs in an impressive variety of rainforest scenery, peaceful walking trails, and one of the most rewarding viewpoints in the country

The trails are well-maintained and easy to follow, making the park suitable for families, seniors, and casual walkers. Along the way, you’ll find lush tropical vegetation, towering trees, interesting plant life, and plenty of shaded paths that make for a relaxing stroll even on warmer days. The shelters and lookout points are thoughtfully placed, offering great spots to rest and soak in the surroundings

The highlight is undoubtedly the former quarry lookout. The emerald-green water framed by dramatic rock cliffs creates a surprisingly scenic landscape that feels far removed from the city

It seems this is my favourite spot to hike and train myself for my next climb. The trail is like super easy so I do many repetitions. The place is nice. Plenty of wild snakes and monkeys to look for. If you are lucky, juvenile king cobras too.
Nice and peaceful park with an easy walking trail. Great for a short stroll, and the quarry view is really nice.
This is an easy trail and accessible via either from car park or Bukit Timah visitor center. That’s a playground for children age 7-12 also. No admission ticket required.

Such a pretty park ! Super near Bukit Timah Summit so it can be a little outing. Went around noon and I was almost fried by the sun. Lowkey got lost but it was relatively easy to find my way to the Quarry ! Recommend to visit in the late afternoon to avoid the sun hehe. Not sure why they say you need to buy tickets but it’s free !!

Very Peaceful and quiet. Should try and book.
If any of u Wana book, pls proceed to Blk 15 RC Center. With Passion Card it's $20. Without Passion Card is $30. Timing wise is from 7pm till 10pm.
Wonderful place to have a nice short walk. There are playgrounds and plenty of room for children to run around. Quiet except can get quite crowded at times but still ok.
Love the sultry fragrance of the Tembusu trees in the Garden when they flower in May-June and October.
The park just renew. Very nice and comfy,,, the kids excited comes along to the park
Cleanliness of the garden needs to be improved, especially the places around the tall playground

New BTO ready on January 2023. Strategic location behind Bukit Panjang Post Office and The Orange Tree Preschool (CCK). 3-4 mins walk to Junction 10 and Pheonix LRT Station. 5-6 minutes walk to Bukt Panjang MRT Station. 7-8 mins walk to Hillion Mall. 1 min walk to Pang Sua PCN and Rail Corridor (CCK).

Best neighbourhood I've ever seen and went to. Well-maintained. Great playground place to play around. Good surroundings. Great and Friendly people. There's a MyFirstSkool childcare in the area, a garden at the multistorey rooftop, and a nearby 7-eleven that opens 24 hours and Rasa Rasa Restaurant which opens till 12am /1am that consists variety of good and tasty foods.
The nearest supermarket will be at Shengshiong at Junction 10 which has a lot of variety of things to get there.
Overall ,the Teck Whye view is a great place and the best place I've been to.

Newly developed bto...a stone's throw away from Rasa Rasa Restaurant (closed when we came in the morning).  So far the playground caters for the young and old...even with a gym-like setting in the middle..remember to wear loose & light clothing as it was scorching when we arrived at 9am with occasional passing clouds providing us with shelter from time to time.  There is also 2 swings for parents to 'heal' while watching them kids play.
Glad that I am part of this new estate's.  Definitely one of the best town for me.
There are high buildings.  Place is quite well. It's beside the Lrt Station

place_name: PCF Sparkletots Preschool @ Yew Tee Blk 690D
review_text: Children celebrating the National Day while parents and guardians looked on

place_name: PCF Sparkletots Preschool @ Yew Tee Blk 690D

review_text:
Greenfield Montessori has been a wonderful school for all my children. My first child transitioned to P1 smoothly and did not struggle with the change. My K2 boy is doing very well in reading and robotics, and my N1 girl went from crying at school to happily dancing her way in and wanting to attend school without tears.

The Principal is very caring and approachable, always checking matters personally and supporting parents closely, especially during the early weeks. The teachers are patient, dedicated, and supportive, and the school environment is safe, calm, and nurturing.

Overall, the program has helped my children grow academically, emotionally, and socially. I highly recommend Greenfield Montessori.

We had a wonderful experience with Greenfield Montessori, and I’m truly grateful for the strong foundation it gave my son. He genuinely enjoyed the lessons and was always excited to go to school. The Montessori approach helped him grow not just academically, but socially and emotionally as well.

He built meaningful friendships with his peers, and those early relationships really boosted his confidence. What speaks volumes to me is that even now, while he’s already in primary school, he still talks about his preschool teacher. That kind of lasting impression says everything about the care and dedication of the educators here.

The teachers at Greenfield Montessori are incredibly passionate and truly care for each child as an individual. They create a nurturing, supportive environment where children feel safe, valued, and encouraged to learn. I highly recommend Greenfield Montessori to any parent looking for a preschool that genuinely puts children first.

Special shout out to Mrs Vasu, the firm yet fun-loving principal who conducts the most engaging lessons, and Teacher Medaline, my boy's favourite teacher who never fails to give him a hug even after he graduated.

My son joined Greenfield Montessori when he was 3 years old, which was 9 years ago. We have been extremely happy with the school ever since. The Montessori program is well-structured and engaging, and the school also offers systematic Math, English, and Chinese curricula, which helped build a very strong foundation for my child.

Thanks to their guidance and support, my son transitioned smoothly into primary school with no learning barriers. His strong foundation enabled him to excel academically, and he successfully entered GEP in Primary 3.

I highly recommend Greenfield Montessori for parents looking for a nurturing environment that prepares children both academically and holistically.

This school has really shaped my son's primitive years before he went to primary school. It was amazing learning with the teachers here. He had his favorites. The school really emphasizes on good balance of play time and studies. Engaging them well, good food and a special mention to Vasugi for all the help and all the support she has given us over the years . The school also laid a great foundation for my son's journey into learning Mandarin and it was amazing how fast he learned at the school. Thank you to Greenfield Montessori for such wonderful years of my son in your school. He is now in year 10 but fondly remembers your school

The principal, teachers and staffs in Greenfield Montessori are caring and really hands-on. We can tell that children do enjoy learning thru variety of methods such as traditional inside classroom, arts, games and outdoor learning, and even simple livelihood activities too. Parents are also given individualized report with updates of the lessons and child participation for the week. I am so glad everytime i hear my child sharing some stories about what she learned at school and some incident that happens with her friends.

MRT with connections! Next to neighborhood Heartland malls like Yew Tee Point with great views and local International delights banking shopping dinning supermarkets cinemas entertainments marketing  learning groceries  sundries postage services and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students tourists Expats visitors locals and families.

It is the nearest station from my home. Buses come to this station are 302,307 and 979.

It is on the Red line with Choa Chu Kang on one side and Kranji on the other.

It has many amenities including wet market ,bank,food courts  and clinics etc.

Yew Tee residents find it easy to get food from  Subway ,Long John Silver ,Stuff'd, Mcdonalds (24 hours),Mala Pot ,Four Leaves, KFC , Yu Kun Kaya Toast, Toast box  and  there are many other options available at the food court at basement.
air conditioner is not very  cool . Nature air reduce the hot.
Pleasant, well-functioning station on the NS line. Clean facilities. Nice view.

Definitely the most reliable station to go to wherever I go beyond my town. However, improvements at the road beside the station and the connections can be improved though I am seeing a lot of improvements in progress. Toilet has been refurnished and looks clean. Pleasing music and staff during peak hours, though the station is getting old. Overall rating: 4.15/5

This station has some shops which serves food and has a NTUC finest supermarket. The facilities in this station is adequate and that the passenger flow is generally fast. There are facilities where people of all disabilities are able to use and it is commuter friendly, overall a good station
Please get a bus interchange for this location and get a bus number for Sungei Kadut, need at least 2 or 3 buses that can go to Sungei Kadut.
Fairly clean station with a few bakeries, great for office workers or for soldiers who work nearby to get their breakfast
Close to my abode, I have been using it for almost fifteen years. Thank you, SMRT, for your nice service.
Excellent
Need at least 5 buses that can go to Sungei Kadut, so that its easier for everyone to travel different buses. Need more shuttle bus for Sungei Kadut st2 Hafary building.
Roy is the best way 😍😂
nice place

Kopitiam Corner is located just outside Yew Tee MRT, second story. Accessible by the slimmest escalator in Singapore, stair, or elevator at the end.

The food court itself has a modern look despite the more traditional surrounding. It has aircons, many stalls and sitting areas. I come here in the mornings, so there are normally only a few stalls open, including the Yong Tau Fu at the corner that I always patronize because it’s an easy choice for me.

The place is clean enough, comfortable because of the controlled climate. There is a public toilet at the corner of the two wings (food court shaped like a L). It looks like there are decent variety of food but there is always a long queue at the drink stall.

It’s pretty easy to find seat in the morning (before 8/9). The food quality is okay, the price is medium (Yong Tau Fu costs around 7 dollar for basic/minimum choice of 6 items + rice/noodle). Location is very convenient because it’s near residential areas, MRT, market, and other amenities.

there's a new stall, seems like its opened by the famous indian zi char chef from bedok, blacky seafood? blecky seafood?

tried the bbq squid rice, its not bad. sauce is okay but guess the squid is blanched then the sauce just added on top as the squid seems to not have absorbed any sauce.

Worker putting fingers into the cups to transfer cups to coffee counter.
Yes worker was wearing gloves but his hands were touching many areas.
This transfer was also done Infront of the customer queue and ppl have to give way to him.
I am sure NTUC has better way to deliver the clean cups to the counter.

i will amend my review to 5 star if corrective action is carried out.
Thank you!

I went to Yew Tee Square today and, as usual, headed to the Kopitiam at Level 2. We had Yong Tau Foo, Popiah, and coffee. The Yong Tau Foo price had risen to S$7.00, but it's quite a big bowl that can be shared by two pax. The coffee was thick and fragrant. The Popiah wasn't too soggy or sweet - it was to my liking.

果汁檔： When you’re juicing other fruits, wash the container first before using it again!
Damn it 🤬🤬🤬 the sugarcane juice I bought tastes like orange juice. Absolutely disgusting 👎👎👎.

This Koufu is located in the basement of Yew Tee Point, which is just a short walking distance from Yew Tee MRT. It's not big, but there are decent enough varieties for customers. We tried the chicken rice. It's not fantastic but okay enough. The place is clean and bright, just like other Koufu branches.

There is another Kopitiam located at the second story of Yew Tee Square, and a small Food In just outside the MRT.

For other F&B option, there are individual food stalls and fast food like McD.

Disappointed with the fish ball noodles stall food standards. Order spare rib prawn soup. Meat used belly meat with 50% fats. Not shown in picture spare ribs with soft bones. Soup is sub par with little prawn taste and tasteless. Very disappointed with such stall selling few variety of noodles, not specialised enough to have unique taste.

I would like to commend the three more senior ladies at the drinks and snacks stall for their consistently efficient service.

They remain calm and attentive at all times, listening carefully to customers’ orders and preparing items with care. They took time to clarify and confirm my orders before arranging for payment.  Across my many visits, including during peak hours, I have not observed any mistakes.

On a side note, the new young lady at the counter is confident and efficient too. When a customer walked away with a cup of coffee that belonged to the next customer, she politely called back the drinks. This is rarely seen in food courts where orders are fast and customers are impatient. They have truly done a great job.  Their boss has keen eyes for great workers.

Although I do not know them personally, I felt it was important to recognise their dedicated service, which always makes my visits pleasant.

On 22 January, in the morning, at the drink stall, the customer placed the order correctly; however, the staff member appeared unable to understand simple Chinese. She then returned to her colleague, spoke negatively about the customer, and laughed.
Such behavior is disrespectful and unacceptable and reflects poorly on customer service standards.

I was at this place again. This time, I ordered Bread Set A, Spinach Fish Soup, and Old Cucumber Soup. I forgot to take a photo of the Bread Set, and both the fish soup and Old Cucumber Soup were taken halfway through. Everything was good, but the Old Cucumber Soup stood out — it was really good and not too salty for my liking. I’ll try other soups next time I’m here.

I was there for lunch today. We ordered Sliced Fish Noodle and Dumplings — I forgot to take a photo — and Korean Kimchi Soup with rice. All of them were to our liking.
Lot of choices but a little pricey. Ate a value meal, Chicken + soup for $8.30 and Tom Yam La Mian at $5.50. The taste to me is just nice.

Food was great for the price. They initially missed out my order, but when I checked and mentioned that I wouldn’t want it anymore if it hadn’t been cooked, the cashier promptly updated me. The service was really good — she even remembered the situation when we were paying, despite the restaurant being packed and a long queue waiting outside. That level of attentiveness really stood out.

April 2026
Ordered medium curry with cheese. And add on $4.9 meat. Next time to order small curry without cheese as it’s quite salty.

The absolute worst service known to Man.

Firstly, the order was delayed. Secondly, the order was totally incorrect. Thirdly, when we tried to call the staff for assistance, we were ignored multiple times and when the staff came, she basically said we were wrong and took off with the incorrect order (despite the order chit saying otherwise).

The food is about the worst quality imaginable, the only redeeming factor was that it is affordable. But I’ve been to many other Saizeriyas with much better customer service than this.

I understand the place is busy, but for God’s sake please at least get the basics right.

I visited the Saizeriya at Yew Tee Point today, and the service was very disappointing. The staff’s attitude from the start was poor, and the food service was slow and disorganized.

We ordered two pastas, two desserts, and one chicken steak. My friend’s chicken steak and the two desserts were served, but our pastas never came — even after waiting over 40 minutes and asking the staff twice to check.

In the end, we only paid for the chicken steak and the two desserts, and the other two items were refunded. It was a frustrating experience. Hope the management can look into improving both the service attitude and kitchen coordination.

visit here quite frequently. food is okay and price is reasonable, service is fast so its an okay place to pop in for a quick fuss-free meal.

will continue to visit

Enjoyed the delicious food here. Like the polite service here too. One person which caught our eyes is this young man. Working non-stop throughout our meal time, which is well over an hour. Can see him working hard like a bee; carrying meals for the customers and takeaways, bring away the dirty utensils, etc. When not serving food, he initiated to do other works, such as top up the cups of the drink bar. Commendable efforts, which institute positive dining experience.

Just had dinner here, and oddly we just found this place while searching around for certain dishes. Saw that the reviews here could point to hits and misses, but guess it was hits for us. The restaurant really has a menu thats spans many cultures, but of course leaning more towards I believe Arab and Turkish roots.

We landed on Dum Mutton Briyani, Butter Chicken with Rice and one of my kids was spoilt for choice as he wanted to try many of the Turkish items but in the end, got the Mousakka with lamb (like a lagsana). I also got the Kalaki under the omelette section which the manager explained was like an omelette with pleasant gravy within. I must admit we loved all the dishes and I really like the papadum that came with the Briyani which was not the typical kind but thicker and crunchier. In the end, it was really filling for all of us. The only gripe I have was that the kopi I got was really sweet, too sweet for my liking, thus would have to get siu dai in future.

Service wise, the staff were all attentive and the manager was friendly. Even when I went to ask for some utensils, the waiter wanted to bring over to my table, but I told him it was ok. We were early around 5, so crowd wise, there were still ample tables. So all in all, a good experience and would definitely be back.

We had a Lamb Maqlubah $24.90++ and Nutella Pancake $12.80++
We had to wait quite awhile for our food with the restaurant about 80% full during lunch on 1st day of CNY. Both were very tasty but the quantity of lamb was too little for the price. Some berries (like in the photo on the website ordering system) would give the pancakes 5 stars! I love that there wasn't too much Nutella in the pancake 😋

Great experience, simple nice decor. Good service and amazing menu range from western, asian to middle eastern food. You could eat here everyday and  still got something new to try.  Food quality is good, reasonable portion and great taste. The lamb was tender and full of flavour. The Hummus could be improved and sauces less oily. Overall, still a good place to hang with family and friends.

Had Mother's Day lunch. Food was awesome. Service was great eventhough it is fullhouse.
Satay, tulang merah and meat platter as so so good. Thank you for the complimentary cake!
Sya was attentive to our request and kudos to all those that working all out.

Has always wanted to try after seeing a lot of good reviews & seeing the pictures on FB. So finally we got to try yesterday. Spacious, cosy & comfortable sitting area. After going thru the menu a few times thinking what to order as there was a widespread of items, we chosen:

*BBQ Mixed Seafood Grill
*Truffle Mushroom Risotto
*Beef Lasagna
*Baby Kailan Daging
*Cheese garlic naan
*Plain naan
*Butter chicken
*Tom yum beef soup
*Chicken Tikka Masala
*Felafel
*Kunafe

We definitely did not regret choosing the above as the taste was so flavorful, succulent & mouth watering!! The portion was huge too. There were so many pieces of beef in the kailan & tomyum. The lasagna has so much mince beef. And service was good too. They offered to join the next table to ours after seeing our table was cramped. And also helped to pack certain items that we couldn't finish.

Overall, it was a wonderful dining experience & we will certainly come back soon to try other dishes. Thank you Syed Care for making our day!!

Yew Tee Point is located outside the Yew Tee MRT Station. It's very easy to miss because of the ongoing renovation that is happening in the middle of the square.

Unlike the square, this place is air-conditioned. It's like a big open space with many things happening in the middle including stalls, merchandize displays, and F&B. It gives the vibe of older style malls but somehow feels comfy.

First time here and will be the last. The security is so rude and stucked up. There is no motorcycle lots at this mall. The security guard shouted "Hello! No parking here". When ask where can park ..simply say "Don't know!" What is so great about this mall??
Fire alarm went off but security do not allow people to take the escalator and leave the basement level. This is highly unusual as it could be an actual fire.
Koufu Hawker center at YewTee Point mall offers a wide range of food options. Seating space is ample and clean. The food range is also very affordable
Hub mall of Yew Tee MRT Station. Has many dining and cafe options. Great!

Located just outside of Yew Tee MRT, Yew Tee Square looks around the same compared to almost twenty-year ago, until now, the hoarding is up to revamp the place.

Personally, I don’t mind the traditional feel and look, because this kind of place is disappearing now, but hopefully, the upgrading also comes with improvement, because this place definitely needs improvement. One of the things on top of my head is benches. There is nowhere to sit! Or just a space to arrange your things. It used to be an open plaza with a plenty of space to put on a bench or two, but there was none of it.

You can find almost everything you need here. There is a food court (Kopitiam Corner) on the second story, accessible by escalator, elevator, and stairs. There is a dental clinic (that exists since decades ago), and a Value $ shop on the same level.

On the ground floor, there is a big McD, and a big wet market that sells everything that can normally be found in wet market. Other amenities include money changer, coin laundry, atms, miscellaneous shop, small makan area next to a fruit stall, even small shops like fashion, incense, shoes, sushi, photo shops, salon etc.

Very strategically located to serve the neighborhood or commuter, walking distance in front of the Yew Tee MRT.

WENT TO THIS SHOP TO GET MY WATCH STRAP FIXED BUT INSTEAD,GETTING A STUCKED UP ATTITUDE FROM THIS LADY WHO WAS NOT HAPPY TO SERVE AND ATTEND SO I RATHER NOT GE IT DONE.
IT'S NOT AS IF IM GETTING IT DONE FOR FREE.TO THE BOSS OF THIS SHOP PLS TEACH UR STAFF SOME MANNERS.
At a Philippine food stall, the dishes are not priced. If she likes some, she'll ask for $8, and if she doesn't, she'll ask for $10.

Attn:Hayley Williams

Back up your claims with proof
When you patronise Yew Tee Square Shopping Centre or anywhere else be respectful Abide by the rules+regulations of the place+comply
Respect the Security of the premises,do not challenge them,do not abuse them

Free entry without TraceTogether scanning makes the mall more crowded with food stalls in increased customer's population. There seems to have the atrium sales everyday with eye-catching clothes and footwears. Kofu food court draws more customers these days.

Excellent place with a wide variety of items and household essentials. Although queues are quite long here, the cashiers are very efficient at clearing the queues. There is also a FairPrice Finest nearby if you can't find any items. The cashiers are also very chatty and friendly too, making the shopping experience more enjoyable!

This is a great place to shop. There are friendly aunties and uncles in this outlet that helps to enhance the shoppers' experience. There are many daily items available in this outlet. It is a fairly small outlet when compared to other outlets. There is both self checkout machines and human cashiers. Take note that they close the self check out machines at around 9.30pm and there remains about 2 cashiers counter open after around 10pm. There appears to be a lack of cashiers during the non peak hours and some counters will be closed. Overall, a fairly good experience at this outlet though it may get crowded at times. To enter the store is quite easy and the check in process takes quite fast.
this NTUC has a small bar inside, can get sushi or other japanese sides to eat with beer at the counter tables. limited seats of 5-6 only but not a bad place to chill for a beer for a short break.

I was excited to get my hands on the cute Milo Plushies, so I headed to the Store.
However, the Cashier informed me that there was a limit of two Cartons per person, which wasn't mentioned in the Promotion Area.
To make matters worse, the Plushies hadn't arrived yet.
According to the Milo promotion it started from April 26, but as of April 27 the stock still hadn't arrived.
I'm wondering if it's the first day of delivery from storage to outlets.

The marketing strategy for Milo's 75th anniversary seems questionable.
A promotion that allows 1O Cartons for $75 via App Delivery would make more sense instead of charging $78.
It would be more appealing to customers.
Limiting purchases at the outlet is probably to prevent scalpers.

As someone with a big family, one carton of Milo only lasts a week.
I'm absolutely disappointed. 😞
the entrance is right in a corner which can easily be missed. but inside is pretty much the standard - decent sized, decently stocked.

this was around 12ish pm just now. i was filling my slurpie cup when it exploded and fell to the ground. i was shocked that happen and the messed that i caused expecially on the floor. your female staff came to me soon after she cleared customer at the cashier side. i told her what happen and she was really really calm despite the messed and advice me how to fill my cup next time! definitely don't put my cup directly on the dispenser as it create no air to escape hence the pressure and explosion! Thank you so much Vivian for your help and being patient and understanding!! you deserve recognition and a RAISE !!

On 6/2/26 at around 7pm, I attempted to open my yuu app when making payment, but the network was weak and it took less than 90 seconds to load. During this time, the cashier (Chinese male with spectacles) showed impatience, sighed loudly and spoke rudely. He then told me it was “too late” to accumulate points and said I should inform him earlier, despite clearly seeing me trying to refresh my app.

This behaviour is unacceptable. Cashiers usually ask customers if they have the yuu app. His attitude was unprofessional and disrespectful. customers should not be expected to tolerate such treatment.

I went arn 545 asking the Indian cashier if there they sell EZ-Link card as the 711 near my block sells ezlink cards as I was in need of one and the cashier rudely told said “don’t have lah” so I asked her if she can give me cash and I Paynow her , and she shouted at me “don’t have la” yes , I know that question abit ridiculous but she can atleast reply properly? What type of service is this ?? Do not go to the 711 unless u want to be treated horribly

One of my favourite place in yew tee. But I can't accept this that it is close?? What does 7 eleven means? It means it operates 24/7 and does not close..

I need my food after come back from a long day. This is not acceptable. U can clean but at 1am? This is not the time.

A regular at this outlet as I live close by, and like the service that the staffs has displayed. But as for this encounter with one of the cashier personnel on 8 July 2025, 1030+ pm.

An Indian lady working at the cashier has bad service attitude. The way she talk is rude and all I wanted was to scan my points but she mentioned that it’s not possible as she has alr click payment method and awaiting for me to pay. But can’t she cancel the transaction and re-do the whole process again? I believe this can be done as long as the payment has not been processed (I did not pay or tap my card yet).

conveniently located for quick snacks and bites. this outlet is huge, clean and neat but selection is not a good as other 7-11s in terms of snacks and candy. also seems like promo updates are a bit slower here.

It's a store and more !! Have a good variety of ready to eat meal and snacks. Also sells a small variety of veggies and fruits. A S'pore Pools outlet is also within the store. Spacious, neat and clean. Friendly staff too.

very rude young indian cashier (male and female) now. unlike the polite cashier before (middle age male).

there was an offer for 3 onigiri rice for $6 but when scanned, it was tally as original price for each. they insisted for me to pay the original price, rather than helping to check. why label an offer but it wasn't entered into the system?

the both young indian male and female can be always seen smoking under sheltered void deck.

i no longer visit this 7 11 outlet. yew tee station outlet do just fine.

Very rude indian cashier. Yesterday i went to purchase sanitary pads for my daughter and i asked for a specific brand. And she said " What got there all there lah" . Is this what you train your staff to say? I wonder how y'all stay in business. Never returning until you do something about her. And ill bring this up to the management soon.

This big sized shop is neatly maintained and had excellent air conditioning system. They do have friendly staffs. It's located very close to bus stop and thus attracts many people for their early morning Coffee/Tea. They are offering good varieties of coffee and tea, should try cappuccino. I suggest them to increase the grocery items because they are having lesser grocery items while comparing with other 7-Eleven in the island. They don't sell any kind of booze drinks. The Singapore pools are located inside this shop which is the predominant reason for crowd pulling.

I love working out at this 24/7 Fitness outlet, and it’s entirely down to the incredible team here. From the manager to the trainers, everyone is exceptionally dedicated, knowledgeable, and approachable. The manager runs a tight ship and ensures the gym stays clean, welcoming, and well-organized, while the trainers genuinely care about your progress and always bring the best energy.
Beyond the fantastic staff, the gym has an incredibly friendly, community vibe and is well-equipped with everything you need for a solid full-body workout. Having such a great crew and facility right here in Yew Tee is a total game-changer. Huge kudos to the whole team—keep up the amazing work!

Excellent gym with great convenience and facilities. The location is very convenient, just about a 30-second walk from the MRT, and it is open 24/7, which makes it easy to fit workouts into any schedule. The gym also has useful facilities such as a water dispenser, showers, lockers, and washrooms.

I have been attending about four times a week, and the equipment is more than sufficient to train all muscle groups efficiently. The gym is well-equipped, accessible, and practical for consistent training. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a reliable 24-hour gym near the Yew MRT and live near CCK area. 😁

Fitness First Yew Tee is clean, well maintained, and has a good range of equipment. The staff and trainers are friendly, helpful, and create a positive training environment. It can get busy during peak hours, but overall it is a great gym to work out at.

Had my first PT session with Pheng today and it was fantastic. I’m basically an auntie who hasn’t moved in years so I’m currently very unfit. He was very patient and calming, and really good at explaining the exercises. Appreciated the advice about diet and protein intake too. :) Great first day back. See you next week!

Really enjoy training at 24/7 Fitness. As a full-time student, I find the membership very affordable and worth it. The gym environment and equipment have really supported both my muscle growth and sports performance. Great place to train consistently and improve yourself.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Yew Tee Community Club
review_text:
Anytime Fitness Yew Tee is well-equipped with all the essentials I need for my workouts, though I do wish there were a wider variety of equipment. The gym is clean and the machines are well maintained, although the air conditioning could benefit from more regular servicing.

The staff are exceptionally friendly, especially Dylan. He is very helpful to me when i need assistance inside the gym, always greeting members warmly and taking the time to chat, which creates a cozy and welcoming, home-like environment. Very supportive guy, readily offering fitness tips and guiding me on how to use unfamiliar equipment. All of the staffs help has made my gym sessions more enjoyable and boosted my confidence to try new exercises.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Yew Tee Community Club
review_text:
Amazing gym with great vibe and staff

Anytime Fitness Yew Tee CC has been one of the best gyms I’ve trained at. The gym is clean, well-maintained, and the equipment is always in good condition. The environment is friendly and not intimidating, which makes it easy to stay consistent.

I also want to give a special shoutout to Dylan, he’s super helpful, friendly, and always brings good energy to the gym. Dylan is the type of person who genuinely makes members feel welcome and comfortable, whether you’re new or experienced. His service and attitude really stand out.

Highly recommend this branch if you’re interested

place_name: Anytime Fitness Yew Tee Community Club
review_text:
I’ve been training at this gym for a while now, and I can confidently say it’s one of the best gym environments I’ve experienced. The equipment is modern, well-maintained, and there’s a great variety for both strength training and cardio. The gym is always clean, spacious, and never feels overcrowded, which makes workouts much more enjoyable.

A special shoutout to Dylan Lui. He is incredibly professional, knowledgeable, and approachable. Dylan takes the time to understand your fitness goals and gives practical, personalised advice to help you improve. Whether it’s correcting your form, planning a workout routine, or simply offering motivation, he goes the extra mile to make sure you feel supported.

What really stands out about Dylan is his positive attitude and genuine passion for helping others. He creates a welcoming atmosphere that makes both beginners and experienced gym-goers feel comfortable and confident.

Highly recommend this gym — and if you get the chance to train with Dylan Lui, you’re in great hands! 💪

place_name: Anytime Fitness Yew Tee Community Club
review_text: Great gym with a clean setup and friendly vibes. Anytime Fitness Yew Tee CC is well-maintained and not too crowded. Shoutout to Dylan — super helpful, friendly, and always gives good training advice. Highly recommend!

place_name: Anytime Fitness Yew Tee Community Club
review_text: Amazing place to gym with its high quality equipment. The staff there are amazing especially Hamkah who is friendly and knowledgeable in the correct forms.

Went there to play badminton. the multi purpose hall is air conditioned. The lighting is very comfortable for the eyes. The Yew Tee CC is located near a bus stop. Really convenient just one stop away from the MRT. Yee Tee CC has a indoor basketball court.

Came here to play badminton with friends and family.  It is a nice community centre located in the heart of the Yew Tee neighbourhood that provides lots of facilities. The Community Centre consists of many different things at the higher levels there are student care services and tuition centres. There is also a multi purpose hall too.

A conveniently located community center, full of amenity and facility for meeting, conference, sports activities and yoga, training class rooms . The better part of it is the co-location with Chua Chu Kang NPC which serves the community with security, law and order.
Very nice and clean. Bright with a high ceiling. Police post sharing building.
Basketball court. Not so crowded on Sunday morning

🛝🪜The Children Playground consist of a ground section where there are Swings and a Climb Structure with slides.
🛝Climbing up to the terrace section, a prominent green camouflage-coloured Long Slide takes centerstage.
🛕The slide is accessible by climbing up the Tower.
🪆🪅Around the base of the Tower are Interactive Play Structure and rounded Stepping Stones designed for smaller children.
🌳The Children Playground is well shaded.
🤹🛝Great place for children to have fun time.

The tall slide playground set and swings are under maintenance.
Small set is still available to play. There is big slide and carousel.
Super fun playground near MRT- my 3 year old climber loved everything and was entertained for over an hour!

The longest slide one can ever find in a HDB estate. The beast is 20m long from a mini mill all the way down to the rest of the playground.  The good old swings also made a comeback. Playground is built by government that meticulously design it to suit kids from 3 years old to 12 years old. I’m proud to stag near this park.

I have been to this park many times with my children but never quite explored the space behind the playground. During this session of “Art by the Park”, the children found many treasures!

To my surprise, there are many interesting plants and trees at this park! Don’t forget to spot the durian tree, the pine cones and more! This space has so much to offer ❤️

When visiting, don’t forget to bring your insect repellent and your sense of adventure!

place_name: The Life Style Corridor - Yew Tee
review_text: The children’s playgrounds and waterplay features, a thematic “Sampan Plaza”, a treehouse playground, a street soccer court, and an event lawn called the “Kampung Pavilion” will be up and running at this place called "Yew Tee Life Style Corridor". A transformation much welcome by the community, especially the younger generation, as they will have new playgrounds to spend their free time.

place_name: The Life Style Corridor - Yew Tee
review_text:
Very exiting and lovable place, especially for children, and I believe adults too.

Fun water theme play area.
Good use of land below the MRT Lines.

place_name: The Life Style Corridor - Yew Tee
review_text: Changed from a boring walkway to a place everyone can play and bomb with, and also it is free for all.

place_name: The Life Style Corridor - Yew Tee

review_text: place is just beside and near the Yawtee MRT. Have water playground and also have shower point and changing rooms. Good to bring child on weekends or weekdays.
How i wish I turn back the clock ⏰️ to be a child again to enjoy this water playground. So inviting. 🤩🥰

Closed for cleaning on Wednesdays 9am-2pm

Simple water play the closest to any MRT

Mall right beside
